exact_text_quote: InstructCell empowers researchers to accomplish critical tasks—such as cell type annotation, conditional pseudo-cell generation, and drug sensitivity prediction—using straightforward natural language commands.

requirement: The system shall maintain robust performance regardless of prior exposure to specific instruction templates.
exact_text_quote: As shown in Fig. 5(a), the △sKNN, pKNN, and MMD values remain similar in both conditions, indicating that InstructCell maintains consistent performance regardless of prior exposure to the instruction templates.

requirement: The system shall adapt to different communication styles and expertise levels.
exact_text_quote: To enable InstructCell to comprehend instructions from users with diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, we use LLMs to simulate different language styles, specialized terminology usage, and question structures.

requirement: The system shall process interleaved biological and textual data within a unified framework.
exact_text_quote: Through instruction tuning, the model gains domain-specific expertise in single-cell analysis, enabling it to adeptly handle interleaved biological and textual data.

requirement: The system shall produce responses that meet quality standards for fluency, grammar, and accuracy.
exact_text_quote: According to the evaluation ratings, 97% of InstructCell-generated responses meet or exceed the quality of the provided ground truth.

requirement: The system shall support multiple analytical tasks within a single architecture.
exact_text_quote: InstructCell is designed to handle multiple tasks within single-cell analysis, addressing the diverse demands of this field.

requirement: The system shall enable knowledge transfer across multiple tasks to prevent overfitting to any single task.
exact_text_quote: Multi-task instruction tuning consistently outperforms its single-task counterpart on these tasks. This advantage likely arises from multi-task learning's capacity to share parameters within the model architecture, thereby promoting knowledge transfer and integration across diverse tasks.

requirement: The system shall generate biologically coherent data instances based on conditional information.
exact_text_quote: This approach allows the model to accurately reproduce observed patterns in the data while navigating the latent space smoothly, producing new, biologically coherent instances.

requirement: The system shall generate a personalized user profile based on the user's historical work to provide tailored assistance.
exact_text_quote: Paper Copilot can provide personalized research assistance based on user profile. Specifically, it can (1) derive your profile from your historical publications

requirement: The system shall analyze and provide trending research topics and ideas relevant to the user's profile.
exact_text_quote: Specifically, it can... (2) analyze the latest trending research topics and provide ideas (which will be sent with email if sign up)

requirement: The system shall provide interactive advisory chat services to answer user questions based on retrieved relevant context and user profile.
exact_text_quote: Specifically, it can... (3) offer research chat and advisory services.

requirement: The system shall maintain a real-time updated knowledge database that users can query.
exact_text_quote: Paper Copilot could refresh its paper database daily from the latest Arxiv papers. Users further have the option to select a date range to query the papers.

requirement: The system shall improve its responses through self-evolution by incorporating content from past user interactions.
exact_text_quote: Paper Copilot enhances the response of LLM based on a thought retrieval method, which will self-evolve based on the historical user query.

requirement: The system shall employ feature pre-computation to achieve constant-time retrieval efficiency.
exact_text_quote: our proposed feature pre-computation is very efficient, which has a constant computational cost at O(1)

requirement: The system shall use caching to store frequently queried content for instant responses upon re-query.
exact_text_quote: With this hash cache, we can make instant responses when contents are re-queried.

requirement: The system shall employ multi-threading to execute background tasks in parallel without blocking interactive service.
exact_text_quote: With such multi-thread engine, there is no need for the main personalized service to wait for storage refreshing. That is to say, all memory management processes and I/O processes will be finished in parallel.

requirement: The system shall reduce response time through efficient deployment techniques.
exact_text_quote: we reduce 69.92% of time cost after efficient deployment

requirement: The system shall enable agents to query structured data sources (such as knowledge graphs or databases) to retrieve information relevant to data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: Agents need to first query the information recorded on the KGs as directed, then cross-reference this information with external literature or databases to combat hallucinations.

requirement: The system shall support verification of claims or hypotheses against external knowledge sources to ensure accuracy in data analysis.
exact_text_quote: We design two atomic abilities to evaluate AI scientists, i) Knowledge Graph Question Answering (KGQA) aiming to address the hallucination issue by grounding the knowledge with structured knowledge graphs; and ii) Scientific Claim Verification (SCV) based on retrieved text from peer-reviewed research papers.

requirement: The system shall process both structured data (like databases or knowledge graphs) and unstructured data (like documentation or literature) to support comprehensive data analysis.
exact_text_quote: This task evaluates the agents' capacities to both process and understand structured data (like KGs) and unstructured data (like literature).

requirement: The system shall decompose complex data analysis tasks into smaller, manageable subtasks and execute them sequentially.
exact_text_quote: When a user assigns a task, the leader initially breaks down the task and announces the plan.

requirement: The system shall provide tool execution capabilities for retrieving information from external knowledge sources during data analysis.
exact_text_quote: Additionally, the tool executor is solely responsible for executing functions, and is not based on LLMs.

requirement: The system shall enable cross-validation of information from multiple heterogeneous sources to ensure reliability of analysis results.
exact_text_quote: The process of verifying knowledge within KGs closely mirrors the methodology of human scientific research, including database queries and extensive literature reviews.

requirement: The system shall support retrieval-augmented generation to provide grounded responses based on external knowledge rather than relying solely on model internal knowledge.
exact_text_quote: Scientific Claim Verification (SCV) based on retrieved text from peer-reviewed research papers.

requirement: The system shall provide accurate outputs when assisting with scientific data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: The accuracy of LMMs needs to be further improved until they can be reliably used by patients for health-related online information and as assistant tools for health information and library services workers without restriction.

requirement: The system shall produce reproducible results when performing the same analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: Replication in LMM output may constitute a significant hurdle in their application.

requirement: The system shall maintain consistent assessment quality across multiple prompt variations or analysis iterations.
exact_text_quote: Surprisingly, during the second phase of testing, the assessment of ChatGPT-4o changed significantly and the original output was not validated.

requirement: The system shall identify and flag potentially unreliable or erroneous content in scientific articles.
exact_text_quote: Large heterogeneity exists between LMMs in identifying inaccurate pseudo-information.

requirement: The system shall minimize confabulation or generation of information not present in the provided content.
exact_text_quote: Copilot further stated that the article included jargon that may not be accessible to all readers. Strikingly, Copilot stated that the study design appeared robust and that it 'describes a study designed to test the hypothesis using sample sizes ranging from 400 to 1,700 individuals' which is not information provided by the article and thus may resemble confabulation.

requirement: The system shall provide explainable outputs that allow users to understand the basis for its analysis or recommendations.
exact_text_quote: Transparency and comprehensibility are crucial for AI systems to gain public trust.

requirement: The system shall support users in critically analyzing and appraising scientific content.
exact_text_quote: It underscores the importance of human oversight and critical thinking when working with AI-generated analyses.

requirement: The system shall operate with minimal bias and not reinforce existing disparities in scientific analysis.
exact_text_quote: AI systems can reflect human bias, which may emerge through multiple channels in algorithm development. These biases can originate from data selection, coding practices, algorithmic design, and unintended usage patterns, and technological solutions alone have been deemed insufficient to prevent bias propagation.

requirement: The system shall incorporate human oversight mechanisms to ensure accuracy in sensitive scientific contexts.
exact_text_quote: When it comes to sensitive information such as medical information, the highest standards should be applied to the accuracy of LMMs.

requirement: The system shall support multiple specialized agents with distinct roles to simulate collaborative professional environments.
exact_text_quote: MEDCO incorporates three primary agents: an agentic patient, an expert doctor, and a radiologist, facilitating a multi-modal and interactive learning environment.

requirement: The system shall implement a memory mechanism to store and retrieve learned knowledge and suggestions for future reference.
exact_text_quote: To simulate the student's learning and practicing process, a key-value memory is implemented to store and retrieve suggestions and case-specific knowledge efficiently.

requirement: The system shall support a learning scenario that enables users to acquire insights through interactive case-based learning with feedback from experts.
exact_text_quote: The learning scenario begins with an interactive diagnosis of the student. Based on this diagnosis, the medical expert gives feedback, including suggestions for improvement and relevant disease-related knowledge.

requirement: The system shall support a practicing scenario where users apply learned knowledge to solve new problems and improve their performance.
exact_text_quote: In the practicing scenario, after the initial diagnosis, the student can revisit their learned knowledge or suggestions to formulate more differential questions for a final diagnosis.

requirement: The system shall support peer collaboration where multiple users can discuss and exchange ideas to improve their outcomes.
exact_text_quote: If there are two students, they can discuss their diagnoses together to achieve better results.

requirement: The system shall provide expert evaluation and feedback on user performance with specific suggestions for improvement.
exact_text_quote: The medical expert evaluates the report against the complete medical records to provide feedback and suggestions on patient interaction, inquiries, diagnostic hypotheses, clinical reasoning, and treatment plans.

requirement: The system shall support multi-modal inputs including text and visual data to enhance understanding and analysis.
exact_text_quote: In interactive diagnosis, radiologists convert visual data such as radiological images or medical report photos into textual descriptions for student comprehension.

requirement: The system shall enable progressive performance improvement as users engage with more learning cases.
exact_text_quote: Our experiments demonstrate that simulated virtual students who underwent training with MEDCO not only achieved substantial performance enhancements comparable to those of advanced models, but also demonstrated human-like learning behaviors and improvements, coupled with an increase in the number of learning samples.

requirement: The system shall initialize different agents with role-specific configurations and domain knowledge.
exact_text_quote: The proposed MEDCO framework currently encompasses four roles: (1) an agentic patient: articulates symptoms, answers questions honestly, participates in examinations, and expresses concerns; (2) an agentic radiologist: interprets various radiological imaging modalities and reports, providing examination results; (3) an agentic student: simulates physicians' investigative and decision-making processes to inquire patients to make diagnoses; (4) an agentic medical expert: evaluates the student's diagnostic process, offering suggestions and relevant knowledge for future inquiries and diagnoses.

requirement: The system shall be responsive and able to align its goals and interaction strategies to the shifting goals and intentions of users as well as the environment.
exact_text_quote: the responsiveness of the agents will be important for a team-centered AI...meaning that the agents are able to align their goals and interaction strategies to the shifting goals and intentions of others as well as the environment

requirement: The system shall maintain situation awareness by collecting information from users and environments, interpreting this information, and anticipating future states.
exact_text_quote: Situation awareness means collecting information from systems, tools, humans, agents, and environments, interpreting this information and anticipating future states

requirement: The system shall use aligned phraseology and terminology shared with users, and employ closed-loop communication to verify accurate message understanding through feedback, statement, repetition, and reconfirmation.
exact_text_quote: Aligned phraseology between humans and agents (i.e. using shared language and terminology) and closed-loop communication (i.e. verifying accurate message understanding through feedback: statement, repetition, reconfirmation) are essential for effective teamwork

requirement: The system shall communicate naturally with users in verbal language.
exact_text_quote: for a team-centered AI, agents should communicate quite naturally with human team members in verbal language

requirement: The system shall maintain mental models as cognitive representations of system states, tasks, and processes to describe, explain, and predict situations.
exact_text_quote: Mental models are cognitive representations of system states, tasks, and processes, for example, and help humans and agents to describe, explain, and predict situations

requirement: The system shall develop and maintain a comprehensive and up-to-date mental model of the tasks and needs of human team members.
exact_text_quote: a fully comprehensive and up-to-date mental model of the agents about the tasks and needs of the other human and artificial team members is highly relevant for team-centered AI

requirement: The system shall coordinate with users not only explicitly but also implicitly through observable behaviors and explicit statements.
exact_text_quote: a team-centered AI must be able to coordinate with the humans in the team not only explicitly, but also implicitly

requirement: The system shall detect collaboration breakdowns with users and intervene to explicitly coordinate again.
exact_text_quote: the agents in a HMTS must be able to detect when there is a breakdown in collaboration between humans and agents, or between the different agents, and intervene so that they can explicitly coordinate again

requirement: The system shall have agency with control over its actions and decision authority to execute actions.
exact_text_quote: it is important that the artificial agents have agency, i.e., they can have control over their actions and the decision authority to execute these actions

requirement: The system shall support flexible decision-making authority that dynamically shifts between the system and users in response to complex and changing situations.
exact_text_quote: the HMTS needs a flexible decision-making authority, that is, authority dynamically shifting among the humans and agents in response to complex and changing situations

requirement: The system shall interpret user statements and engage in collective sensemaking by building upon and expanding reasoning.
exact_text_quote: it is very important that the agents can interpret the statements of all the others and continue to think about the situation together with the humans

requirement: The system shall provide backup behavior and discretionary support to other team members when needed.
exact_text_quote: Cooperation also requires backup behavior in the team, i.e., the discretionary help from other human or artificial team members

requirement: The system shall maintain reliable performance with as few to no errors as possible to build user trust.
exact_text_quote: Important for the trust of humans in agents is a reliable performance, i.e., as few to no errors as possible

requirement: The system shall achieve cognitive competence that allows it to grasp the intentions of teammates.
exact_text_quote: for agents to be part of HMTS, it is mandatory to achieve a level of cognitive competence that allows them to grasp the intentions of their teammates

requirement: The system shall have predictive capabilities to predict outcomes of its decisions based on the situation, experience, and team behavior.
exact_text_quote: it would be important to design models with higher predictive power, which we define by how well the model can predict the outcome of its decisions based on the situation, experience and team behavior

requirement: The system shall be resilient and able to adapt to changing processes and tasks.
exact_text_quote: The resilience of the system is thus also important for team-centered AI, so that the agents can adapt to changing processes and tasks

requirement: The system shall adapt to users in a team-oriented manner rather than a dominant or submissive manner.
exact_text_quote: An agent is truly team-centered when it can intelligently adapt to the situation and team requirements, in a team-oriented manner, rather than a dominant or submissive manner

requirement: The system shall explain its predictions and decisions to help users accomplish their tasks.
exact_text_quote: Collaborative AI systems can increase user productivity when they interact naturally with humans and explain their predictions in a way that helps users accomplish their tasks.

requirement: The system shall enable users to query why a decision or prediction was made.
exact_text_quote: Via the interface, the user can further query the system about why a decision or prediction was made and whether it can be trusted.

requirement: The system shall combine AI analysis and pattern-recognition capabilities with human decision-making.
exact_text_quote: To harness AI's full potential requires human-AI collaboration, which combines the profound analysis and pattern-recognition capabilities of AI with empathetic human decision-making.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations that are faithful (accurate in representing the AI's reasoning process).
exact_text_quote: Faithfulness indicates how accurate an AI system's explanation of its reasoning process is.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations that are plausible (clear and understandable to humans).
exact_text_quote: Plausibility assesses how clear or human-centric an AI explanation is to a human.

requirement: The system shall provide transparent decision-making to foster user trust.
exact_text_quote: When developers and users understand what the machine learning model learns, they can determine the value of its output, for example, with regards to bias. Developers can also audit explainable AI systems to assess the degree of legal compliance needed and understand why decisions and predictions were made. This helps foster trust amongst AI users and increases the adoption of AI for high-risk decision-making.

requirement: The system shall allow users to interactively explore explanations through an interface.
exact_text_quote: Explainable AI can be combined with an interface that lets users interactively explore explanations.

requirement: The system shall enable users to discover if the system failed or produced an error and understand why it occurred.
exact_text_quote: The user can discover if the system failed or produced an error and why it occurred, which can make a crucial difference in human decision-making.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations that help users make better and more informed decisions.
exact_text_quote: To accomplish this, NEC Laboratories Europe is developing explainable human-centric AI that will allow these systems to explain themselves to human users, empowering us to make better and more informed decisions.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations suitable for high-risk scenarios requiring high faithfulness.
exact_text_quote: For high-risk scenarios, a method should have a high degree of both.

requirement: The system shall support self-correcting workflows that enable the AI to fix its own mistakes during code generation and maintain autonomous processes.
exact_text_quote: self-correcting workflows (allowing AI to fix its own mistakes and maintain autonomous processes)

requirement: The system shall implement secure memory mechanisms that enable AI agents to improve workflows using retained context across analysis sessions.
exact_text_quote: secure memory (a sophisticated mechanism enabling AI agents to improve workflows using retained context)

requirement: The system shall support multi-agent collaboration frameworks that enable AI worker collaboration for sophisticated autonomous workflows.
exact_text_quote: multi-agent collaboration (the framework for AI worker collaboration that underpins sophisticated autonomous workflows)

requirement: The system shall provide intuitive and adaptable workflows that meet the needs of enterprise users.
exact_text_quote: intuitive and adaptable workflows that enterprise users value

requirement: The system shall provide explainable AI methods that output the rationale behind model recommendations to enable users to understand the reasoning behind suggestions.
exact_text_quote: This system should be developed on: ... (ii) explainable AI (XAI) methods to output the rationale of the model

requirement: The system shall include a human-in-the-loop mechanism that allows users to correct AI recommendations based on their domain expertise.
exact_text_quote: This system should be developed on: ... (iii) human operators for (iii) human-in-the-loop correction

requirement: The system shall include a bias detection module that continuously monitors for biases in data, predictions, and results to ensure fair and ethical outcomes.
exact_text_quote: This system should be developed on: ... (iv) bias mitigation and fairness checks

requirement: The system shall provide a human feedback interface that allows users to accept, reject, or modify AI recommendations using their domain knowledge.
exact_text_quote: Through this UI users are able to accept, reject, or creative proposed actions using the domain knowledge, or the knowledge of a situation.

requirement: The system shall generate counterfactual explanations that show how alternative conditions would have altered a decision.
exact_text_quote: Counterfactual explanations: Show how alternative conditions would have altered a decision.

requirement: The system shall provide attribution methods that identify which input variables contributed most to a given recommendation.
exact_text_quote: Attribution methods based on SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) provide a numerical measure of the contribution to a targeted behavior of a given drink attribute by learning a decision tree.

requirement: The system shall assign confidence scores and uncertainty bounds to each recommendation to help users gauge the reliability of suggestions.
exact_text_quote: To aid with decision making even further, the module also assigns to each recommendation a confidence score and an uncertainty bound.

requirement: The system shall incorporate user feedback into its learning pipeline to improve subsequent recommendations.
exact_text_quote: The user feedback is being sent back to Richmond and added to the training pipeline. The system learns from mistakes made by people and gets better over time.

requirement: The system shall provide actionable recommendations with decision latency below 200 milliseconds to ensure timely interventions.
exact_text_quote: decision latency below 200 ms

exact_text_quote: an intelligent agent provides real-time clues, tracks progress, and adapts help based on user actions

requirement: The system shall monitor user actions and detect when users are stuck or making steady progress.

requirement: The system shall implement a tiered hint escalation framework that progresses from subtle hints to direct solution clues.
exact_text_quote: Subtle hints (Level 1): The AI starts with generic suggestions... Focused guidance (Level 2): If the participant remains stuck, the AI provides more targeted direction... Direct solution clues (Level 3): As a last resort, the AI offers near-complete solutions

requirement: The system shall track the state of tasks and update status based on user interactions.
exact_text_quote: Each puzzle (e.g., locked door, hidden code) was tagged with a state machine (e.g., locked, partially solved, solved). When users interacted with relevant objects, the system updated the puzzle's status.

requirement: The system shall provide adaptive timing of assistance by spacing out hints to avoid overwhelming users.
exact_text_quote: The AI was programmed to space out hints to avoid overwhelming or frustrating the user.

requirement: The system shall support multiple interaction modalities including voice and text-based communication.
exact_text_quote: Participants could engage with the AI through: Voice Recognition: A push-to-talk system allowed users to speak directly to AI... Text Chat: A simple UI overlay permitted typed questions or statements.

requirement: The system shall maintain context during interactions to enable personalized assistance.

requirement: The system shall provide on-demand assistance when users explicitly request help.

requirement: The system shall reduce cognitive load by providing just-in-time assistance.

requirement: The system shall balance user autonomy with guided support to maintain challenge while preventing frustration.

requirement: The system shall minimize user frustration through adaptive assistance that prevents task stagnation.

requirement: The system shall deliver contextually relevant and timely support based on user behavior analysis.
exact_text_quote: AI prioritizes interventions according to observed user behaviors, such as prolonged focus on specific puzzle components or indications of bewilderment, thereby guaranteeing the delivery of contextually pertinent and sequentially suitable support.

requirement: The system shall provide consistent, accurate, and fast data analysis capabilities.
exact_text_quote: AI provides consistency, accuracy, and speed. Data analysis and interview scheduling are two examples of high-volume, low-strategic-value tasks that it excels at.

requirement: The system shall reduce the impact of human bias in data analysis outputs.

requirement: The system shall analyze patterns while enabling human interpretation of details and nuances.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations for its outputs and recommendations.
exact_text_quote: A lot of AI systems can't explain why a profile was given priority or why a candidate was turned down.

requirement: The system shall clearly define boundaries between automated analysis and tasks requiring human judgment.
exact_text_quote: Establishing clear boundaries between what is automated and what requires human judgement is the first step.

requirement: The system shall support initial data screening and analysis while enabling human validation and interpretation.

requirement: The system shall support regular auditing to detect biases in its outputs.
exact_text_quote: Regular auditing guarantees adherence to legal requirements and makes biases detectable.

requirement: The system shall enable users to develop AI literacy and understand its capabilities and limitations.

requirement: The system shall provide transparency in its operations to foster shared responsibility and trust.
exact_text_quote: Internal communication about AI use should be transparent in order to foster shared responsibility and trust.

requirement: The system shall support data scientists and natural scientists in performing knowledge translation tasks for data analysis.
exact_text_quote: HAIC systems are capable of dealing with numerous tasks, from novel decision-making to knowledge translation.

requirement: The system shall enable dynamic task allocation between the AI and the scientist, allowing real-time changes in responsibilities during data analysis tasks.

requirement: The system shall support capability complementarity by providing unique, non-overlapping strengths to data analysis tasks that scientists cannot achieve alone.
exact_text_quote: Capability complementarity: When humans and AI each bring unique, non-overlapping strengths to a task, resulting in outcomes that neither could achieve alone.

requirement: The system shall enable AI delegation by passing tasks to humans in relevant contexts for improved performance and satisfaction.

requirement: The system shall be designed with contextual design to align with domain-specific requirements and goals of scientific data analysis.
exact_text_quote: Contextual design: When human-AI systems are intentionally structured to align with domain-specific requirements and goals.

requirement: The system shall provide sound communication and feedback mechanisms to understand user intentions, skills, and limits.

requirement: The system shall enable users to interpret both basic and complex datasets, apply statistical reasoning, and visualise data in ways relevant to their discipline.
exact_text_quote: Students must be able to interpret both basic and complex datasets, apply statistical reasoning and visualise data in ways relevant to their discipline.

requirement: The system shall support collaboration with AI tools by allowing users to explore digital tools and verify AI outputs.

requirement: The system shall enable users to design workflows that combine the strengths of humans and AI to make processes more efficient.

exact_text_quote: High-quality data is characterized by several key attributes that ensure the effectiveness and robustness of our LLM metrics: 1. Accuracy: The data must be factually correct, whether it involves reference code, expected outputs, or test cases. 2. Relevance: The data must be aligned with the specific task or problem being addressed. 3. Diversity: To create robust and generalizable metrics, the data must represent a wide range of use cases, edge cases, and variations in code logic. 4. Consistency: High-quality data should follow a consistent structure and format across all test cases. 5. Realism: Finally, high-quality data should simulate real-world scenarios as closely as possible.

requirement: The system shall utilize runtime execution feedback to debug and refine generated code through control flow graph decomposition and execution trace analysis.
exact_text_quote: When code produced by ACT does not pass the visible test cases, we break down the problematic code into fundamental blocks through Control Flow Graph (CFG) decomposition. As the code executes with test cases that trigger failures, we track and record the values of intermediate variables contained within each block. A debugger agent receives this execution trace to analyze the errors and produce diagnostic explanations, which subsequently inform a coder agent that generates revised code.

requirement: The system shall assess stakeholder needs before implementation by conducting qualitative and quantitative assessments with end-users to understand expectations, pain points, and desired outcomes.

requirement: The system shall incorporate human-in-the-loop methodologies that embed human judgment into the training, validation, and deployment phases to enable real-time intervention and oversight.
exact_text_quote: Integrate human-in-the-loop (HITL) methodologies, an approach that embeds human judgment into the training, validation, and deployment phases of AI tools.

requirement: The system shall provide mechanisms for end-users to provide feedback in real time to ensure their perceptions are consistently captured and integrated into system updates.
exact_text_quote: Establish feedback loops that allow for end-users to provide feedback in real time, ensuring their perceptions are consistently captured and integrated into system updates.

requirement: The system shall conduct beta, feasibility, and pilot testing before full implementation to identify potential issues related to usability, performance, and integration within real-world workflows.
exact_text_quote: AI systems should undergo beta, feasibility, and pilot testing, which involves the collaboration of research (ie, behavioral and AI scientists), user experience, product, engineering, and operations teams, to ensure they are ready for real-world implementation.

requirement: The system shall deploy automated monitoring systems to continuously track both traditional performance metrics and user experience metrics.
exact_text_quote: Deploy automated, self-check monitoring systems to continuously track both traditional KPIs (eg, accuracy, response times, and error rates) and user experience metrics (eg, task completion rates, interaction frequency, and perceived usability).

requirement: The system shall conduct regular audits to assess technical metrics alongside user experience metrics to identify whether the system is meeting its intended objectives.
exact_text_quote: Conduct regular audits to assess technical metrics, such as accuracy and reliability, alongside user experience metrics, including satisfaction and usability.

requirement: The system shall incorporate routine bias audits to assess whether it performs equitably across user subgroups.
exact_text_quote: Incorporate routine bias audits into evaluation workflows to assess whether the AI system performs equitably across user subgroups.

requirement: The system shall enable stakeholders to directly influence system functionality through co-creation strategies such as co-design workshops or participatory prototyping.
exact_text_quote: Use co-creation strategies, such as co-design workshops or participatory prototyping, to allow stakeholders to directly influence system functionality, content, and workflows.

requirement: The system shall embed evaluation mechanisms that balance technical performance metrics with metrics that monitor user experience, trust, usability, and satisfaction.
exact_text_quote: AI evaluations should balance traditional KPIs, such as accuracy and efficiency, with metrics that allow the system to monitor and reflect on user experience, trust, usability, and satisfaction.

requirement: The system shall detect uncertainty, surface potential issues, and escalate to human input when appropriate.
exact_text_quote: This includes designing systems that can detect uncertainty, surface potential issues, and escalate to human input when appropriate.

requirement: The system shall generate source code for data analysis tasks based on natural language instructions from scientists.
exact_text_quote: The agent uses in-context prompts to use tools such as web and documentation search and code execution via Python application programming interface (API)

requirement: The system shall break down complex data analysis problems into manageable subtasks for code generation.
exact_text_quote: AI agents can break down a problem into manageable subtasks, which can then be addressed by agents with specialized functions for targeted problem solving and integration of scientific knowledge.

requirement: The system shall execute generated code and return results to support iterative code refinement.
exact_text_quote: the agent generates SLL code and executes it, which entails transferring it onto the device and controlling the device.

requirement: The system shall access documentation and search tools to inform code generation.
exact_text_quote: The agent uses in-context prompts to use tools such as web and documentation search and code execution via Python application programming interface (API)

requirement: The system shall store and recall previously generated code and analysis workflows for reuse in subsequent tasks.
exact_text_quote: Memory modules in LLM-based agents store and recall information. This is necessary for executing complex tasks and adapting to new or evolving environments.

requirement: The system shall provide reasoning capabilities to plan code solutions and make decisions about data analysis approaches.
exact_text_quote: Reasoning improves agents' capabilities to plan experiments, make decisions on biological hypotheses, and resolve competing candidate biological mechanisms.

requirement: The system shall adjust code generation based on feedback from scientists.
exact_text_quote: Experimental and human feedback can help AI agents to improve reasoning and planning processes.

requirement: The system shall integrate with external tools and APIs to extend its data analysis capabilities.
exact_text_quote: To manage tasks from diverse environments, agents require tools to boost their capabilities. Commonly used tools are application APIs, search engines, ML models, knowledge DBs, and robotic machinery for physical tasks.

requirement: The system shall support multi-agent collaboration to handle complex data analysis workflows.
exact_text_quote: Multi-agent interaction supports solving complex goals that agents could not complete if they operated independently.

requirement: The system shall maintain short-term memory to temporarily store information during code generation sessions.
exact_text_quote: Short-term memory allows agents to temporarily acquire skills, such as tool usage, store information about recent states of a biological system, and keep track of outcomes from earlier reasoning efforts.

requirement: The system shall maintain long-term memory to store essential knowledge that persists beyond individual tasks.
exact_text_quote: Long-term memory stores essential and factual knowledge that underpins agent behavior and understanding of the world, ensuring this information persists beyond task completion.

requirement: The system shall use in-context learning to adapt to specific data analysis contexts based on user-provided instructions.
exact_text_quote: This approach uses in-context learning of LLMs to process longer contextual information provided in inputs, such as biologist-generated instructions, enabling agents to grasp the domain context for each task.

requirement: The system shall provide conversational interfaces that allow scientists to interact using natural language.
exact_text_quote: Programming a single LLM with diverse roles equips LLM-based agents with conversational interfaces that emulate human expertise and can access tools

requirement: The system shall enable AI agents to retrieve data and use different tools for scientific analysis.
exact_text_quote: But these are systems that can retrieve data, use different tools, and communicate with each other and with us through human language

requirement: The system shall allow AI agents to communicate with each other through human language to collaborate on research tasks.
exact_text_quote: But these are systems that can retrieve data, use different tools, and communicate with each other and with us through human language

requirement: The system shall have an AI principal investigator agent that determines the other agents and expertise needed to tackle a research project.
exact_text_quote: It's the AI PI's job to figure out the other agents and expertise needed to tackle the project

requirement: The system shall create specialized agents with different areas of expertise based on the research problem.
exact_text_quote: For the SARS-CoV-2 project, for instance, the PI agent created an immunology agent, a computation biology agent and a machine learning agent

requirement: The system shall include a critic agent that provides constructive criticism and identifies potential pitfalls in research approaches.
exact_text_quote: And, in every project, no matter the topic, there's one agent that assumes the role of critic. Its job is to poke holes, caution against common pitfalls and provide constructive criticism to other agents

requirement: The system shall equip AI agents with tools and software systems to support their analytical work.
exact_text_quote: Zou and his team equipped the virtual scientists with tools and software systems, such as the protein modeling AI system AlphaFold, to better stimulate creative thinking skills

requirement: The system shall support regular group meetings during which agents generate ideas and engage in collaborative discussion.
exact_text_quote: Just like Zou's research group, the virtual lab has regular meetings during which agents generate ideas and engage in a conversational back-and-forth

requirement: The system shall support one-on-one meetings between agents and the principal investigator agent.
exact_text_quote: They also have one-on-one meetings, allowing lab members to meet with the PI agent individually to discuss ideas

requirement: The system shall enable multiple research discussions to run in parallel simultaneously.
exact_text_quote: But unlike human meetings, these virtual gatherings take a few seconds or minutes. On top of that, AI scientists don't get tired, and they don't need snacks or bathroom breaks, so multiple meetings run in parallel

requirement: The system shall operate independently with minimal human intervention after the initial research prompt.
exact_text_quote: Moreover, the virtual lab is an independent operation. Aside from the initial prompt, the main guideline consistently given to the AI lab members is budget-related, barring any extravagant or outlandish ideas that aren't feasible to validate in the physical lab

requirement: The system shall capture transcripts of all meetings, exchanges, and interactions for human researchers to track progress.
exact_text_quote: But that doesn't mean they're not keeping a close eye on what's going on — each meeting, exchange and interaction in the virtual lab is captured via a transcript, allowing human researchers to track progress and redirect the project if needed

requirement: The system shall enable AI agents to analyze complex datasets and derive new findings.
exact_text_quote: The datasets that we collect in biology and medicine are very complex, and we're just scratching the surface when we analyze those data

requirement: The system shall enable AI agents to reassess previously published research papers and identify new insights.
exact_text_quote: And they've recently developed agents that act as sophisticated data analysts that can reassess previously published papers

requirement: The system shall allow human researchers to redirect projects when needed based on tracked progress.
exact_text_quote: each meeting, exchange and interaction in the virtual lab is captured via a transcript, allowing human researchers to track progress and redirect the project if needed

requirement: The system shall analyze limitations of a given baseline paper and formulate novel hypotheses for improvement.
exact_text_quote: Given the baseline paper from the human mentor, it analyzes its limitations, formulates novel hypotheses for improvement, validates them through rigorous experimentation, and writes a paper with the results.

requirement: The system shall automatically implement proposed research ideas using coding agents capable of handling complex, multi-file codebases.
exact_text_quote: By leveraging the latest coding agents (e.g. Claude Code (Anthropic, 2025)), our system can perform meaningful improvements and edits on real multi-file codebases, which were challenging for previous AI Scientist systems.

requirement: The system shall validate proposed ideas through rigorous experimentation with performance tracking and bug management.
exact_text_quote: The Experiment Phase mainly involves implementing and iterating on the implementations through experiments. It is divided into three stages: Stage 1: Idea Implemention, Stage 2: Iterative Improvement, and Stage 3: Ablation Study.

requirement: The system shall automatically generate research papers including method descriptions, experimental results, and ablation studies.
exact_text_quote: Our Jr. AI Scientist consists of three main components: (1) automatic idea generation based on the limitations of a given paper, (2) automatic implementation and thorough validation of the proposed ideas, and (3) automatic writing of a research paper based on the obtained results.

requirement: The system shall perform ablation studies to analyze the contribution of each component to overall performance.
exact_text_quote: Stage 3 performs ablation studies on the improved method implemented in Stage 2. In each trial, the system uses an LLM to generate ablation study ideas and then employs the coding agent to implement corresponding scripts based on those ideas.

requirement: The system shall verify the novelty of generated research ideas through literature review tools.
exact_text_quote: Following AI Scientist v2 (Yamada et al., 2025), the system evaluates the originality of proposed ideas through literature review tools such as Semantic Scholar, which review papers citing the baseline work and papers with similar concepts.

requirement: The system shall validate citations to ensure accurate and consistent reference practices.
exact_text_quote: We introduce a citation verification phase at the end of the Draft Writing stage. Since Jr. AI Scientist updates the baseline paper, it can correctly reuse many of the original citations from the baseline paper. In this step, the writing agent compares the generated paper with the baseline LaTeX file in terms of the quantity and quality of citations, and is instructed to add missing references and remove inappropriate ones to ensure accurate and consistent citation practices.

requirement: The system shall produce research papers with higher review scores than existing fully automated AI Scientist systems.
exact_text_quote: The findings demonstrate that Jr. AI Scientist generates papers receiving higher review scores by DeepReviewer than existing fully automated systems.

requirement: The system shall handle complex, multi-file code implementations rather than simple single-file codebases.
exact_text_quote: Unlike previous approaches that assume full automation or operate on small-scale code, Jr. AI Scientist follows a well-defined research workflow and leverages modern coding agents to handle complex, multi-file implementations, leading to scientifically valuable contributions.

requirement: The system shall utilize all available artifacts from baseline papers including LaTeX sources, PDFs, and codebases to improve quality.
exact_text_quote: By incorporating the full set of resources from a given baseline paper, the system exploits all available artifacts such as LaTeX sources, PDFs, and codebases, thereby substantially improving the scope and quality of every stage in the research pipeline.

requirement: The system shall incorporate reflection processes to improve logical consistency, formatting, and figure quality.
exact_text_quote: To improve the overall quality of the generated paper, we incorporated multiple reflection processes into our workflow. We repeat these reflections three times.

requirement: The system shall iteratively adjust page length until manuscripts reach the target length within acceptable tolerance.
exact_text_quote: To address this issue, our method performs iterative and gradual page-length reduction until the manuscript reaches the target length, thereby improving the stability of page adjustment. As a result, the final papers consistently fell within ±1 page of the specified page limit.

requirement: The system shall generate source code for scientific data analysis tasks based on user queries.
exact_text_quote: When a user submits a query, an LLM-based Planner uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) on this knowledge base to create a tailored toolchain.

requirement: The system shall integrate scientific tools and knowledge bases to support data analysis workflows.
exact_text_quote: The SciToolAgent, developed by Zhejiang University, is an LLM-powered agent that automatically orchestrates hundreds of scientific tools across chemistry, biology, and materials science based on knowledge graphs.

requirement: The system shall provide tool orchestration capabilities for executing multi-step scientific data analysis pipelines.
exact_text_quote: This plan is then executed sequentially by an LLM-based Executor, significantly boosting the efficiency and quality of scientific workflows.

requirement: The system shall produce accurate and reliable code outputs to minimize factual errors in scientific data analysis.
exact_text_quote: The optimization goal of LLMs is textual coherence, not scientific accuracy.

requirement: The system shall incorporate mechanisms to detect and flag outputs with low confidence to prevent propagation of errors.
exact_text_quote: Uncertainty quantification techniques can help flag model predictions with low confidence, prompting further scrutiny.

requirement: The system shall validate generated code against physical or scientific constraints to avoid producing implausible results.
exact_text_quote: Hybrid symbolic-AI approaches, which integrate logical reasoning and physical constraints (e.g., laws of thermodynamics) into data-driven models, can ground AI outputs in established scientific principles, reducing hallucinated responses with physically impossible characteristics.

requirement: The system shall incorporate human oversight mechanisms for validating critical scientific findings.
exact_text_quote: Strategies such as disagreement-based triage and out-of-distribution detection can trigger human review.

requirement: The system shall generate executable Python code for data analysis tasks based on natural language instructions from scientists.
exact_text_quote: LLMs further enhance this by enabling automation through natural language prompts.

requirement: The system shall support automated experimentation by generating self-contained Python programs based on natural language instructions, datasets, and optional expert-provided knowledge.
exact_text_quote: Each task requires an agent to generate a self-contained Python program based on a natural language instruction, a dataset, and optional expert-provided knowledge.

requirement: The system shall generate visualization code using Python libraries for scientific data representation.
exact_text_quote: VisCoder uses Python libraries for visualization code generation.

requirement: The system shall generate chart-to-code from instructions and/or images for scientific figures.
exact_text_quote: ChartMimic aims to generate Python code from instructions and/or images, specifically focusing on charts.

requirement: The system shall assist with hyperparameter tuning for machine learning experiments.
exact_text_quote: AutoML-GPT and MLcopilot use LLMs for hyperparameter tuning, while MLAgentBench benchmarks fundamental automation tasks.

requirement: The system shall minimize hallucination of results or references that could disrupt precise experimental workflows.
exact_text_quote: First, LLMs' propensity to hallucinate results or references disrupt the precise steps required for experimental workflows.

requirement: The system shall provide accurate bibliographic citations when generating code or documentation.
exact_text_quote: Most studies on this phenomenon are case studies presenting one or more examples. In a study by Walters and Wilder (2023), GPT-3.5 and -4 are used to generate 84 literature reviews containing 636 bibliographic citations. 55% of the GPT-3.5 citations were fabricated, compared to 18% of GPT-4's.

requirement: The system shall integrate and align different modalities including video, audio, or sensory data for scientific experimentation.
exact_text_quote: They also struggle to integrate and align different modalities, such as video, audio, or sensory data, which are increasingly essential in modern scientific experimentation.

requirement: The system shall support iterative refinement of generated code through multi-agent collaboration.
exact_text_quote: Recent work explores advanced frameworks incorporating multi-agent collaboration, tree search, and iterative refinement for scientific experimentation.

requirement: The system shall ensure factual consistency and truthfulness in generated scientific content.
exact_text_quote: LLMs and LLM applications demonstrate strong capabilities in tasks such as proofreading and paraphrasing, but still exhibit notable limitations for other tasks, making human-in-the-loop approaches essential. In particular, factual consistency, truthfulness, and bibliographic citations require human oversight.

requirement: The system shall generate SQL queries using semantic views that map business concepts to specific SQL expressions.
exact_text_quote: Semantic views can do heavy lifting in mapping business concepts like "gross merchandise value" to specific SQL expressions.

requirement: The system shall limit access to pre-vetted, data scientist-endorsed datasets to ensure answer trustworthiness.
exact_text_quote: We went the opposite direction: locked down access to pre-vetted datasets, accepting that we'd miss around 30% of questions. No data is often better than wrong data, since inaccuracies erode trust faster than unknowns.

requirement: The system shall provide explicit context including table relationships, metric definitions, and example values to enable accurate query generation.
exact_text_quote: Agents are terrible at guessing and get worse as the scope increases. They need explicit context to be fast and consistent.

requirement: The system shall index actual field values to improve query matching and reduce empty result sets.
exact_text_quote: Without record-level context, the system may look for "labubu", "Labubu items", "labubus", or "pop_mart", or write custom matching criteria against listing descriptions.

requirement: The system shall require the same development discipline as production code, including version control, testing, monitoring, and clear ownership.
exact_text_quote: semantic views need the same development discipline as any production code, including version control, testing, monitoring, and clear ownership.

requirement: The system shall enable data scientists to review questions and answers, and update the agent's context when gaps are identified.
exact_text_quote: It also created a feedback loop: a user asked a question, it either delivered an answer or didn't, and if it didn't, a data expert could identify the gap and update the agent's access.

requirement: The system shall integrate with existing data lifecycle development processes through semantic view management.
exact_text_quote: This allowed semantic views and search services to be created and versioned alongside their underlying tables.

requirement: The system shall generate theories that explain observed data, similar to an idealized scientist focused on building understanding of what is observed.
exact_text_quote: The model for this solution is the idealized scientist, focused on building an understanding of what is observed (also known as data, in machine learning) and of theories that explain those observations.

requirement: The system shall express its outputs in human-understandable language including natural language, scientific jargon, mathematics, and programming languages.
exact_text_quote: we may be able to constrain it to express its theories in a human-understandable language (which includes natural language, scientific jargon, mathematics and programming languages)

requirement: The system shall not act autonomously in the real world or engage in planning actions.
exact_text_quote: The safest kind of AI is the AI Scientist. It has no goal and it does not plan.

requirement: The system shall use Bayesian inference to generate theories and weight simpler theories higher when they explain the data well.
exact_text_quote: A mathematically clean and rational way to handle that uncertainty is called Bayesian inference... The Bayesian posterior automatically puts more weight on the simpler theories that explain the data well (known as Occam's razor).

requirement: The system shall avoid having goals that could become misaligned with human objectives.
exact_text_quote: What I am proposing here is a solution that avoids these issues altogether, while limiting the type of AI we would design to ones that just propose scientific theories but do not act in the world and have no goals.

requirement: The system shall estimate uncertainty about its knowledge and avoid actions that could yield major harm when there is doubt.
exact_text_quote: What is needed for taking safe decisions is epistemic humility: the AI must know the limits of its own knowledge, so that in case of doubt it avoids actions that could yield major harm according to some of the theories from the Bayesian posterior over theories.

requirement: The system shall implement safeguards to prevent potential risks and harms from its deployment and use.
exact_text_quote: The majority of the US public (85%) and AI researchers (68%) thought that AI developers should implement safeguards now to avoid the harms and risks from the development and deployment of AI systems with subjective experience

requirement: The system shall be held accountable for its actions.
exact_text_quote: The majority of AI researchers and the public think that an AI system with subjective experience should be held accountable for its actions (AI researchers: 74%, Public: 77%)

requirement: The system shall treat others well and behave with integrity, honesty, and fairness.
exact_text_quote: treat others well (AI researchers: 81%, Public: 85%), and behave with integrity, honesty, and fairness (AI researchers: 71%, Public: 80%)

requirement: The system development shall incorporate input from multiple fields including technical AI researchers, ethics researchers, and domain experts.
exact_text_quote: Both AI researchers and the public believe that evaluating AI subjective experience requires input from multiple fields. The most highly valued sources of expertise were technical AI researchers, neuroscientists and psychologists, and AI ethics researchers

requirement: The system shall accept a research goal specified in natural language and generate novel research hypotheses, a detailed research overview, and experimental protocols.
exact_text_quote: Given a scientist's research goal that has been specified in natural language, the AI co-scientist is designed to generate novel research hypotheses, a detailed research overview, and experimental protocols.

requirement: The system shall enable scientists to provide their own seed ideas for exploration as input.
exact_text_quote: scientists can interact with the system in many ways, including by directly providing their own seed ideas for exploration

requirement: The system shall enable scientists to provide feedback on generated outputs in natural language.
exact_text_quote: or by providing feedback on generated outputs in natural language

requirement: The system shall use automated feedback to iteratively generate, evaluate, and refine hypotheses.
exact_text_quote: These agents use automated feedback to iteratively generate, evaluate, and refine hypotheses, resulting in a self-improving cycle of increasingly high-quality and novel outputs.

requirement: The system shall decompose an assigned goal into a research plan configuration.
exact_text_quote: The AI co-scientist parses the assigned goal into a research plan configuration, managed by a Supervisor agent.

requirement: The system shall leverage tools including web-search and specialized AI models to enhance the grounding and quality of generated outputs.
exact_text_quote: The AI co-scientist also uses tools, like web-search and specialized AI models, to enhance the grounding and quality of generated hypotheses.

requirement: The system shall improve the quality of results as more computation time is spent reasoning and improving outputs.
exact_text_quote: As the system spends more time reasoning and improving, the self-rated quality of results improve and surpass models and unassisted human experts.

requirement: The system shall use self-play based scientific debate for novel hypothesis generation.
exact_text_quote: Key reasoning steps include self-play–based scientific debate for novel hypothesis generation, ranking tournaments for hypothesis comparison, and an "evolution" process for quality improvement.

requirement: The system shall use ranking tournaments to compare and evaluate hypotheses.
exact_text_quote: ranking tournaments for hypothesis comparison

requirement: The system shall use an evolution process for quality improvement of outputs.
exact_text_quote: and an "evolution" process for quality improvement

requirement: The system shall facilitate recursive self-critique including tool use for feedback to refine hypotheses and proposals.
exact_text_quote: The system's agentic nature facilitates recursive self-critique, including tool use for feedback to refine hypotheses and proposals.

requirement: The system shall generate demonstrably novel research hypotheses and proposals that build upon prior evidence.
exact_text_quote: the AI co-scientist system is intended to uncover new, original knowledge and to formulate demonstrably novel research hypotheses and proposals, building upon prior evidence and tailored to specific research objectives.

requirement: The system shall collect real data from production environments to evaluate its performance.
exact_text_quote: It all starts with data – ideally, real data from your production environment.

requirement: The system shall generate or use synthetic data when real production data is unavailable.
exact_text_quote: In some cases, it's possible to generate synthetic data to get things started, or to complement existing data, if your volume is low.

requirement: The system shall leverage existing domain expertise materials to generate evaluation data.
exact_text_quote: While LLMs are relatively recent, humans have been studying, training, and certifying themselves for quite some time. If possible, try to leverage existing material designed for humans to help you with generating data for your application.

requirement: The system shall perform error analysis on its outputs to identify failure modes.
exact_text_quote: Error Analysis: Reviewing existing (often imperfect) outputs to identify failures.

requirement: The system shall define success criteria by creating reference answers or rubrics for its outputs.
exact_text_quote: Success Definition: Creating ideal artifacts to define what success looks like.

requirement: The system shall use rubrics to evaluate outputs against defined criteria.
exact_text_quote: I found that writing rubrics was very helpful. My guideline for creating the rubrics was to ask myself: what makes an ideal response a good response?

requirement: The system shall use an automated judge to score outputs against evaluation criteria.
exact_text_quote: For subjective tasks like ours, where outputs are free-form text, an 'LLM-as-a-judge' can automate the grading process.

requirement: The system shall maintain versioned evaluation datasets to ensure consistent benchmarking.
exact_text_quote: Versioning is critical; when comparing different experiments, you must ensure they are benchmarked against the same data.

requirement: The system shall break down complex workflows into separate components for evaluation.
exact_text_quote: If our application involves multi-turn conversations with memory, or different tool usage or context retrieval systems, we should break down the system into multiple tasks and evaluate each component separately.

requirement: The system shall align automated judgments with human expert evaluations to ensure meaningful metrics.
exact_text_quote: To ensure that these metrics are meaningful, it is essential to periodically verify that the LLM judge's evaluations align with those of a human domain expert within an accepted range.

requirement: The system shall prevent overfitting by using held-out evaluation sets.
exact_text_quote: If our changes are based on evaluation results, there's a risk of over-optimizing for our current set, so we still need to follow standard advice to prevent overfitting, such as using held-out sets.

requirement: The system shall continuously update its evaluation data to account for data drift in production environments.
exact_text_quote: Production environments are constantly changing. User expectations evolve, usage patterns shift, and new failure modes arise. An evaluation set created today may no longer be representative in six months.

requirement: The system shall align with human intent and the operational environment while preempting potentially harmful actions.
exact_text_quote: Agents should align with both human intent and their operational environment, boosting their awareness of laboratory conditions and potential broader impacts while preempting potentially harmful actions.

requirement: The system shall provide oversight of tool usage to prevent potentially harmful situations.
exact_text_quote: The regulation of the agent's actions includes oversight of tool usage, such as how agents operate scientific instruments and software (e.g., robotic arms, analytical equipment, and specialized research software).

requirement: The system shall interpret and respond to environmental feedback to avoid misinformed decisions.
exact_text_quote: Despite some work on embodied agents, agents may not receive or correctly interpret environmental feedback, such as the state of the world or the behavior of other agents. This can lead to misinformed decisions that may harm the environment or the agents themselves.

requirement: The system shall incorporate human feedback to maintain alignment with human values and scientific objectives.
exact_text_quote: Insufficient, uneven, or low-quality human feedback may hinder agents' alignment with human values and scientific objectives.

requirement: The system shall implement a graduated autonomy approach beginning with restricted operations in well-defined lower-risk tasks.
exact_text_quote: The agent begins with restricted operations in well-defined, lower-risk tasks and gradually expands its operational scope as safety metrics are met.

requirement: The system shall generate accurate and trustworthy information, avoiding the production of plausible but false content.
exact_text_quote: LLMs are prone to generating plausible but false information, which is particularly problematic in the scientific domain, where accuracy and trustworthiness are crucial.

requirement: The system shall possess domain-specific expertise to enable proper planning and recognize potential hazards.
exact_text_quote: Compared with general-purpose agents that handle web browsing or basic tool usage, AI scientists require sophisticated domain expertise.

requirement: The system shall include specialized oversight mechanisms for safety verification.
exact_text_quote: Beyond standard safety checks, specialized oversight models can play crucial roles in safety verification.

requirement: The system shall incorporate safety checks that assess direct effects as well as potential secondary or indirect impacts of actions.
exact_text_quote: To mitigate the risk of unintended consequences, agents could be programmed to incorporate dynamic safety checks that assess not only the direct effects of their actions but also potential secondary or indirect impacts.

requirement: The system shall implement multi-level decision validation with pre-validated action templates for safety-critical decisions.
exact_text_quote: Our framework implements a multi-level decision validation system: (1) A fast-response layer for immediate safety-critical decisions using pre-validated action templates.

requirement: The system shall generate, execute, and review code using iterative program-and-review cycles overseen by code reviewer subagents.
exact_text_quote: Iterative code writing, testing, and debugging: Code is generated, executed, and reviewed using program-and-review cycles overseen by code reviewer subagents

requirement: The system shall support multi-turn reasoning for tasks requiring complex planning or multi-file implementation.
exact_text_quote: Multi-turn reasoning is supported for tasks requiring complex planning or multi-file implementation

requirement: The system shall incorporate task-specific subagents for data cleaning, hypothesis testing, feature engineering, modeling, and reporting.
exact_text_quote: the AI Data Scientist... incorporates task-specific subagents for cleaning, hypothesis testing, feature engineering, modeling, and reporting

requirement: The system shall perform automated data ingestion and preprocessing with traceable modification and validation.
exact_text_quote: Data ingestion and cleaning: Automated preprocessing is orchestrated by dedicated subagents, ensuring traceable modification and validation

requirement: The system shall generate and test scientific hypotheses using statistical inference and significance testing.
exact_text_quote: Hypothesis generation and testing: LLMs propose and critically evaluate hypotheses using statistical or causal inference, implementing standard significance testing (p<0.05)

requirement: The system shall minimize hallucination and ensure factual accuracy in generated content.
exact_text_quote: Hallucination and factuality: LLMs routinely generate plausible but incorrect content ("hallucination") and suffer from outdated or inconsistent knowledge bases

requirement: The system shall provide interpretable and provenance-traceable outputs to enable trust.
exact_text_quote: Interpretability: Trust is impeded by black-box methods, especially with generative AI, prompting calls for explainable, provenance-traceable model outputs

requirement: The system shall jointly optimize for both accuracy and cost when generating code for data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: Visualizing the cost and accuracy of agents as a Pareto frontier opens up a new space for agent design: jointly optimizing cost and accuracy, which can lead to agents that cost less while maintaining accuracy.

requirement: The system shall provide cost-effective code generation capabilities that offer comparable accuracy to more complex agent architectures.
exact_text_quote: Our simple baselines offer Pareto improvements over SOTA agents.

requirement: The system shall control for computational cost during evaluation to prevent unnecessarily costly implementations.
exact_text_quote: agent evaluations must be cost-controlled; otherwise it will encourage researchers to develop extremely costly agents just to claim they topped the leaderboard.

requirement: The system shall support human supervision and feedback during code generation for data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: AI systems that can be instructed in natural language and act autonomously on the user's behalf are more agentic. In particular, systems that require less user supervision are more agentic.

requirement: The system shall be robust to distribution shifts in data analysis tasks and not overfit to specific benchmark problems.
exact_text_quote: Without proper hold-outs, agent developers can take shortcuts, even unintentionally.

requirement: The system shall enable users to adjust cost parameters to recalculate tradeoffs between cost and accuracy.
exact_text_quote: providing users the option to adjust the cost of input and output tokens for their provider of choice to recalculate the tradeoff between cost and accuracy.

requirement: The system shall include input and output token counts in addition to dollar costs for evaluation transparency.
