It excels in providing context-aware, real-time code suggestions based on developer input, enhancing productivity.

requirement: The system shall maintain human accountability by ensuring users retain control over reviewing and approving AI-generated outputs.
exact_text_quote: Users retain full control and accountability. This collaboration ensures mutual reinforcement and ethical co-evolution, with AI amplifying human values and capabilities while respecting the human's ultimate authority.

requirement: The system shall use policy signals (risk, task complexity, user expertise level), live system metrics (latency, token cost), and outcome feedback to decide among autonomous generation, tool augmented responses, or human collaboration.
exact_text_quote: The orchestrator uses policy signals (risk, user intent, account tier), live system metrics (latency, token cost), and outcome feedback to decide among autonomous generation, tool augmented responses, or agent collaboration.

requirement: The system shall support four collaboration states (Autonomous, Assistive, Supervised, and Escalated) with policy-driven transitions triggered by calibrated thresholds on risk and uncertainty.
exact_text_quote: The framework exposes four collaboration states-Autonomous, Assistive, Supervised, and Escalated-and a policy that triggers state transitions at calibrated thresholds on risk and uncertainty.

requirement: The system shall provide inline citations and attribution surfaces linking generated outputs to supporting evidence.
exact_text_quote: A co-pilot interface surfaces inline citations, span level attributions from the reranker, structured action proposals, and one click approvals for high risk operations.

requirement: The system shall enforce minimum coverage gating to ensure generated outputs are grounded in retrieved evidence.
exact_text_quote: Groundedness & hallucination control: Minimumcoverage gating; citationfirst prompting; refusal patterns when evidence is insufficient.

requirement: The system shall log decision rationales, latency distributions, token costs, coverage metrics, and escalation reasons to support compliance audits and iterative tuning.
exact_text_quote: An observability layer continuously logs decision rationales, latency distributions, token/call costs, coverage metrics, and escalation reasons to support compliance audits and iterative tuning.

requirement: The system shall provide structured action proposals with predicted impact and required approval levels for high-risk operations.
exact_text_quote: Action proposals: Structured summaries of intended API calls with parameters, predicted impact, and required approvals; one click Approve/Revise/Reject.

requirement: The system shall operate within defined latency budgets (e.g., 200ms or 500ms service level objectives).
exact_text_quote: Latency budgets: 200/500 ms; measure P50/P95 and deadline miss rate.

requirement: The system shall enforce cost controls through token budgets per user tier and adaptive resource allocation.
exact_text_quote: Cost controls: Token budgets per tier; early-exit decoding; selective RAG for low-risk intents; adaptive summarization granularities.

requirement: The system shall maintain safety through guardrails including toxicity filters, PII detection, and policy vetoes.
exact_text_quote: Safety filters: Toxicity/PII/financial risk classifiers; prompt and tool use sanitizers.

requirement: The system shall provide audit trails with cryptographic hash chains for tamper-evident logging.
exact_text_quote: Auditability: Every turn stores: inputs, features, prompts, model outputs, tool I/O, approvals, and final message. A cryptographic hash chain can be enabled for tamperevident logs.

requirement: The system shall support automation of structured, repetitive tasks to improve work efficiency and reduce human labor in routine data analysis operations.
exact_text_quote: Automation aims to improve efficiency and productivity by minimizing the need for human labor in repetitive or predictable tasks

requirement: The system shall provide augmentation capabilities that assist users in decision-making tasks rather than fully replacing human judgment.
exact_text_quote: augmentation involves interaction between humans and AI to execute tasks collaboratively

requirement: The system shall reduce human calculation errors in data processing tasks through automated computation.
exact_text_quote: applying automated AI assistants to decision-making tasks can help reduce human calculation errors to some extent

requirement: The system shall allow users to configure and customize AI behavior according to their specific requirements and methods.
exact_text_quote: the case company allowed users to configure their AI assistants according to their specific business requirements and solutions

requirement: The system shall provide decision support by recommending options while leaving final decisions to human users.
exact_text_quote: the augmented AI recommended some orders for users but left the final decision up to humans

requirement: The system shall maintain user sense of control and avoid making users feel replaced by AI.
exact_text_quote: I hope AI can be my assistant, and we can work together rather than completely replacing me

requirement: The system shall provide transparency in AI decision-making to enable users to understand and verify AI outputs.
exact_text_quote: users often feel compelled to understand and verify the AI's decisions to ensure decision accuracy

requirement: The system shall enable users to understand the logic behind AI recommendations to learn from AI expertise.
exact_text_quote: when users try to understand the logic and functions of the AI assistant in handling a specific business task, they can learn the methods employed by business experts

requirement: The system shall support flexible combination of different AI functionalities to address varied user needs.
exact_text_quote: Users can flexibly combine different functionalities of AI assistants to help them solve various business problems in a personalized manner

requirement: The system shall provide transparent explanations of its decision-making processes and operational logic to users.
exact_text_quote: Transparent algorithms facilitate users' understanding of the operational dynamics and decision-making processes of AI systems, thereby fostering better comprehension and trust in the algorithms' decisions.

requirement: The system shall enable users to comprehend how it processes data and generates code outputs.
exact_text_quote: Algorithmic transparency signifies the degree to which individuals can comprehend, interpret, and predict the operational processes and outcomes of AI algorithms.

requirement: The system shall minimize the black-box nature of its algorithms to ensure users can understand its operational principles.
exact_text_quote: The absence of algorithmic transparency can potentially lead to negative impacts on user experience.

requirement: The system shall support collaborative workflows where AI and human users work together toward shared analytical objectives.
exact_text_quote: Human-AI Collaboration refers to a form of AI application in which humans and AI collaborate closely to achieve a shared objective.

requirement: The system shall leverage computational capabilities to complement human cognitive abilities in data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: Human-AI Collaboration capitalizes on human cognitive abilities and AI computational capabilities, resulting in more efficient work processes and improved outcomes.

requirement: The system shall provide clear attribution of responsibility for its outputs and recommendations.
exact_text_quote: Attribution of responsibility pertains to the cognitive processes individuals employ when assessing and attributing reasons for events or outcomes.

requirement: The system shall ensure that users can comprehend the workings and decision-making processes to maintain collaborative effectiveness.
exact_text_quote: If users are unable to comprehend the workings and decision-making processes of artificial intelligence, it can adversely affect collaborative effectiveness and user experience.

requirement: The system shall assist users in generating creative solutions by enhancing their divergent and convergent thinking abilities.
exact_text_quote: Cluegivers' divergent thinking abilities were aided through using suggestions from the Agent as starting points to further iterate upon.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations for its suggestions to help users understand the reasoning behind recommendations.
exact_text_quote: We chose to show this explanation step to users as players from our pilot testing found it helpful for clue generation.

requirement: The system shall allow users to iterate and refine generated suggestions rather than requiring acceptance of initial outputs.
exact_text_quote: While it was rare that Cluegivers simply accepted the Agent's suggestions, most used the suggestions as starting points to generate improved clues

requirement: The system shall serve as an equalizing tool that helps novice users perform closer to experienced users.
exact_text_quote: The AI acted as an equalizer between novice and more experienced players. It did this by making aspects of the game easier for players who were newer or struggling, and by making it more difficult for players looking to excel.

requirement: The system shall provide psychological support by acting as a safety net that reduces user stress and anxiety during task performance.
exact_text_quote: This participant appreciated the Agent's presence in alleviating some of that initial stress; 'even if I could see a couple of the Agent options are wrong, it's easier to pick say 'out of these three this one is the best' and I can probably modify it [. . . ] so it made it a little bit less stressful as the spymaster.'

requirement: The system shall not overly constrain users' ability to form mental models of their collaborators or teammates.
exact_text_quote: The presence of the Agent drew Cluegivers' attention away from forming mental models of their teammates, instead focussing them on forming mental models of how the Agent would give clues.

requirement: The system shall support users in performing housekeeping tasks such as tracking relevant information and managing task state.
exact_text_quote: Even the explanation text reminded participants that their clues needed to avoid unwanted associations; 'Then also in its explanation it's like 'ohh and it avoids these words by the way, just letting you know I'm avoiding these words' which reminds me, Oh yeah I've gotta be doing that'.

requirement: The system shall balance competence levels to maintain user engagement rather than making tasks too easy.
exact_text_quote: Participants felt there was a fine line that the Agent had to toe between being so good that it defeats the challenge of the game and so poor that it no longer contributes anything to play.

requirement: The system shall enable users to collaborate with unexpected or unconventional suggestions that can inspire creative thinking.
exact_text_quote: poor technical performance from the AI seemed to strengthen the social relationship between the participants. Another study by Hwang and Won. investigated whether perceived identity and conversational style of the AI would moderate the creative outcome of human-AI collaborators in a creative writing task.

requirement: The system shall minimize hidden information that makes it difficult for users to understand the origin and reasoning behind suggestions.
exact_text_quote: Players pointed out that Guessers lack access to explanations when given clues so letting the explanation determine whether Cluegivers give a clue is a path to failure.

requirement: The system shall generate AI suggestions on-demand when explicitly requested by the user, rather than automatically providing them.
exact_text_quote: users need to actively request AI suggestions as it was shown that providing examples on-demand is more effective than automatically providing examples

requirement: The system shall generate suggestions without requiring task-specific training or model re-training.
exact_text_quote: With GPT-3, a generative large language model was developed that outperforms traditional models in many of those tasks but does not require task-specific training

requirement: The system shall provide suggestions that users can review, select, and optionally modify before acceptance.
exact_text_quote: I chose what to select and what not to.

requirement: The system shall generate diverse suggestions to prevent users from experiencing fixation on limited directions.
exact_text_quote: We suggest to employ existing literature on fixation for developing such socio-technical systems.

requirement: The system shall be transparent about its limitations and not imply it has additional task-specific information beyond what is provided.
exact_text_quote: we suggest to make transparent that the system does not have additional task information

requirement: The system shall condition its output on user-provided input to generate relevant suggestions.
exact_text_quote: We include d human ideas into the prompt, as one brainstorming rule is to build upon other’s ideas (Osborn, 1953) and the AI could thus consider the ideas generated by the human.

requirement: The system shall increase users' awareness of their own and others' analytical processes during data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: MAI should increase learners' awareness of their own and others' learning process.

requirement: The system shall support the externalization of users' analytical processes and facilitate sharing and interaction among collaborators.
exact_text_quote: MAI should support the externalization of students' and others' learning process in a social plane and helping in sharing and interaction.

requirement: The system shall prompt the acquisition and activation of regulatory processes to help users monitor and adapt their data analysis approaches.
exact_text_quote: MAI should prompt the acquisition and activation of regulatory processes.

requirement: The system shall be designed so that users perceive it as competent and dependable in supporting their analytical work.
exact_text_quote: Interactions with MAI should be designed so that learners perceive it as competent and dependable.

requirement: The system shall leverage multimodal data to provide timely, adaptive support for users' analytical workflows.
exact_text_quote: the findings highlight the critical importance of multidisciplinary knowledge in the design of multi-agent interfaces (MAI) that provide real-time, adaptive support for group metacognitive processes and decision-making.

requirement: The system shall be responsive to triggers indicating when users need regulatory support and adaptable in reinforcing users' analytical capabilities.
exact_text_quote: advocating for systems that are responsive not only to initial triggers but also adaptable in tuning AI models to reinforce human regulatory capabilities

requirement: The system shall support human-AI collaboration by allowing users to select and refine AI-generated code outputs, as human intervention improves the quality of AI-generated content.
exact_text_quote: The results showed that the beauty rating of the AI-generated haiku with the human intervention was the highest, and those of the human-made and AI-generated haiku without human intervention were equal.

requirement: The system shall generate code that is indistinguishable in quality from human-written code, as AI-generated content can achieve equivalent quality to human-created content.
exact_text_quote: participants could not distinguish between human-made and AI-generated haiku

requirement: The system shall account for algorithm aversion, where users may attribute higher quality work to humans rather than AI, by providing transparency about AI-generated content.
exact_text_quote: a negative correlation was found between discrimination performance and beauty rating in AI-generated haiku, suggesting that high-quality AI-generated work is believed to be human-made

requirement: The system shall adapt to user experience levels, as users with more domain experience demonstrate better discrimination ability between human and AI contributions.
exact_text_quote: the higher the anthropomorphic tendency of animism and the more haiku experience, the higher the correct response

requirement: The system shall enable direct interaction between the human and AI, allowing both to make decisions and perform actions, rather than limiting the human to monitoring or approving AI recommendations.
exact_text_quote: Interactive: both human and AI can make decisions and perform actions.

requirement: The system shall involve the human in all phases of the task execution, ensuring they are directly engaged in decision-making and action-performing rather than excluded from the genuine task.
exact_text_quote: In these paradigms, humans are excluded from a (large) proportion of the genuine task.

requirement: The system shall be designed to allow the human to be the primary party who explains the rationale behind decisions to relevant stakeholders, rather than having the AI explain independently.
exact_text_quote: in the Interactive paradigm, the ratings were more towards the lower values implying the human should be the explaining actor.

requirement: The system shall be designed such that the human receives praise or penalty for outcomes when they are actively involved in the collaboration, rather than distributing accountability equally between human and AI.
exact_text_quote: in the Interactive paradigm, the human receives the praise or the penalty.

requirement: The system shall present the AI as a teammate to the human rather than as a subordinate or tool, enabling collaborative task completion.
exact_text_quote: the perception of AI as a teammate rather than a subordinate was associated with feelings of meaningfulness at work.

requirement: The system shall provide transparency and explainability regarding its actions and decisions to build trust and reduce uncertainty between human and AI collaborators.
exact_text_quote: Transparency and explainability in actions and decisions, and accountability for outcomes, reduce uncertainty and build trust between team members.

requirement: The system shall assign tasks to the human that they perceive as meaningful sources of skill development and task significance, rather than delegating only repetitive or uninteresting tasks.
exact_text_quote: the distribution of tasks between human and AI collaborators has a significant effect on the perception of meaningfulness.

requirement: The system shall ensure that the human has clear visibility into how tasks are performed by the AI to avoid ambiguity and maintain their perception of accountability.
exact_text_quote: Excluding individuals from tasks assigned to AI or keeping them unaware of how those tasks are performed can create ambiguity, reduce their perception of accountability, and consequently contribute to a responsibility gap.

requirement: The system shall enable bidirectional learning where the human user learns from AI-generated outputs and the AI system learns from human inputs during collaboration.
exact_text_quote: the co-learning process is a dual-pathway that both human and AI adapt to each other and learn through their collaborative experience

requirement: The system shall improve individual human performance when the user works independently after collaborative interaction with the AI.
exact_text_quote: Human can learn from the human-AI collaboration experience, leading to an improvement in the individual performance when operating independently after the collaboration

requirement: The system shall improve AI performance when operating independently after learning from human inputs during collaboration.
exact_text_quote: AI can learn from the human-AI collaboration experience, leading to an improvement in the individual performance when operating independently after the collaboration

requirement: The system shall enable alignment between human and AI mental models through collaborative experience.
exact_text_quote: After experiencing the human-AI collaboration, human and AI become more aligned with each other, achieving a closer mental model

requirement: The system shall provide AI feedback mechanisms (recommendations, explanations, or both) that support human learning without causing overconfidence.
exact_text_quote: providing AI explanation makes participants more confident when they work alone in the testing phase

requirement: The system shall support collaborative workflow designs that balance cognitive engagement without reducing AI learning effectiveness.
exact_text_quote: the two-step workflow design reduces the valuable information provided by human-AI collaboration

requirement: The system shall adapt feedback and workflow designs based on user cognitive reflection capabilities to prevent intervention-generated inequalities.
exact_text_quote: the intervention-generated inequality effect exists in both groups with different human cognitive reflection capabilities and within the two co-learning pathways

requirement: The system shall provide a collaborative experience even when both human and AI have moderate performance to trigger co-learning.
exact_text_quote: in our study context, where human and AI have comparable and moderate accuracy, we find that the human-AI collaboration experience can trigger both pathways of the co-learning process

requirement: The system shall not assume that more complex AI feedback or workflow designs automatically produce better learning outcomes.
exact_text_quote: naively deploying a complex AI system without optimizing the design of AI feedback and collaborative workflow could not only fail to enhance their collaboration but also negatively affect the co-learning process

requirement: The system shall calibrate user confidence appropriately during collaboration to prevent overreliance or overconfidence after collaboration ends.
exact_text_quote: withdrawing AI support in the testing phase makes participants significantly less confident in their annotations

requirement: The system shall assist data scientists and natural scientists in processing large amounts of data to discover potential trends and opportunities for innovation activities.
exact_text_quote: AI assists employees in processing large amounts of data and analyzing them, helping enterprises discover potential trends and opportunities, thus guiding enterprises' innovation activities

requirement: The system shall enable collaboration between human researchers and AI to improve productivity in knowledge work tasks.
exact_text_quote: the collaboration between human workers and artificial intelligence in management tasks can effectively improve productivity, emphasizing the collaborative model of close cooperation between human and artificial intelligence in future knowledge work

requirement: The system shall provide data processing and analysis capabilities to support human decision-making.
exact_text_quote: Data processing and model analysis capabilities of AI provide important support for human decision makers

requirement: The system shall automate repetitive and low-value tasks to free human researchers for more innovative and strategic work.
exact_text_quote: AI, can undertake the task of some repeatability and low value, releasing more innovative and strategic HR work

requirement: The system shall combine human cognitive advantages with AI algorithmic advantages to create complementary capabilities.
exact_text_quote: By combining human cognitive advantages with AI algorithmic advantages, they can constantly discover and create new user needs and continuously develop innovation opportunities

requirement: The system shall support analytical decision-making methods for complex data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: While AI systems support analytical decision-making methods, they are less able to understand common-sense situations

requirement: The system shall provide context-aware assistance that incorporates the developer's code and data analysis context to improve task completion rates.
exact_text_quote: LLM-powered assistance significantly boosts task completion rates when it can effectively incorporate a developer's code as context

requirement: The system shall provide context-aware explanations and highlight uncertain AI-generated code to improve user trust and verification.
exact_text_quote: Tools should provide context-aware explanations and highlight uncertain AI-generated code to improve user trust.

requirement: The system shall support verification through automatic test generation, retrieval of reference examples, and static analysis capabilities.
exact_text_quote: Verification support concentrates on tools that help users check and repair outputs before adoption. Proposals include automatic test generation and retrieval of reference examples

requirement: The system shall provide adaptive responses fine-tuned to user expertise and task context to prevent over-reliance on AI assistance.
exact_text_quote: Users need adaptive, fine-tuned to expertise or business context AI responses, and scaffolded AI guidance to prevent over-reliance

requirement: The system shall minimize context switches and reduce boilerplate coding to enhance developer productivity.
exact_text_quote: These improvements are partially attributed to fewer context switches and reduced boilerplate coding, enabling developers to offload repetitive tasks to AI while focusing on higher-level logic.

requirement: The system shall support data analysis workflows including data wrangling, plotting, and notebook-based analysis.
exact_text_quote: Data analysis tasks become more common over time. Studies add wrangling, plotting, and notebook workflows, which reflects the maturity of AI support in these environments and the relevance of studying such use.

requirement: The system shall provide explanations at decision time to support calibrated acceptance of AI-generated code.
exact_text_quote: Explanation and control must be available at decision time. Useful aids in verification include surfacing runtime values, side-by-side alternatives, retrieval of reference examples, static analysis, and unit tests inside the editor

requirement: The system shall address correctness, security, and readability concerns in AI-generated code.
exact_text_quote: Across these papers, the central pattern is a trade-off between speed and assurance. Assistance can accelerate progress, yet LLM-generated code that passes superficial checks may still contain logical flaws or insecure patterns, which shifts effort from authoring toward checking

requirement: The system shall express clear preferences or plans for data analysis tasks rather than solely relying on user direction.
exact_text_quote: An LLM that operates with Agency might volunteer knowledge in the form of expressed preferences (e.g., 'Should we design a chair with wooden legs?')

requirement: The system shall provide reasoning and evidence to motivate its suggestions for data analysis approaches.
exact_text_quote: To have higher Agency, we motivate our intentions through reasoning and evidence. Without such motivation, intentions are simply ideas, often lacking the capability to cause a change.

requirement: The system shall demonstrate self-belief in its judgments and persist with its recommendations even when users propose alternatives.
exact_text_quote: Another factor that contributes to one's Agency is the self-belief one has in their intentions. When one has a strong sense of self-belief, they are more likely to be persistent with their intentions.

requirement: The system shall be able to monitor, adjust, and adapt its recommendations based on new information or feedback.
exact_text_quote: In situations when an individual's initial intentions may not be optimal, it is necessary to monitor, adjust, and adapt them. Such self-adjustment allows better control over one's goals.

requirement: The system shall exhibit strong Intentionality by expressing clear preferences for data analysis methods and code implementations.
exact_text_quote: High Agency requires a strong intention, that includes plans or preferences for a task.

requirement: The system shall exhibit strong Self-Efficacy by pursuing its preferred approach for multiple turns even after the user argues against it.
exact_text_quote: We characterize strong self-efficacy as pursuing a preference for multiple turns even after the other person argues against it (e.g., 'I understand your point of view, but I still prefer the blue color')

requirement: The system shall exhibit strong Self-Regulation by changing to a different approach on its own initiative or compromising when initial approaches may not be optimal.
exact_text_quote: We characterize strong self-regulation as changing to a different preference on one's own (e.g., 'How about using the beige color instead?') or compromising one's preference (e.g., 'Let's compromise and design a beige-colored chair with a brown cushion')

requirement: The system shall measure Agency levels and its four features (Intentionality, Motivation, Self-Efficacy, Self-Regulation) in dialogue interactions.
exact_text_quote: Our goal is to measure (a) Agency, (b) Intentionality, (c) Motivation, (d) Self-Efficacy, and (e) Self-Regulation of each user in a dialogue.

requirement: The system shall be designed to allow modulation of Agency levels based on task requirements and user preferences.
exact_text_quote: The ideal Agency of an agent would be defined by the task/application. Moreover, varying degrees of Agency might need to be manifested at different points in the interaction with a human.

requirement: The system shall adapt its Agency-related constructs using domain-independent stylistic features to permit adaptation across various domains.
exact_text_quote: Although our dataset is focused on the domain of interior design, the Agency-related constructs that we introduce in this paper (e.g., Intentionality) may be associated with domain-independent pragmatic features (e.g., 'I would prefer') and potentially permit adaptation to a variety of domains.

requirement: The system shall provide transparency about its capabilities and limitations to enable users to understand the AI's error boundaries and judge the correctness of generated outputs.
exact_text_quote: the importance of transparency of the AI to the human, i.e., being able to interpret the outputs of the AI, know the error boundaries of the AI, and, ultimately, judge the correctness of the output generated

requirement: The system shall provide preparatory information to users before task execution, including guidelines and examples that improve human performance in code generation tasks.
exact_text_quote: by providing guidelines and examples before the task, studies indicate that human-AI team performance could be increased significantly

requirement: The system shall enable explainability of its code generation decisions to help users comprehend the AI's actions and reasoning.
exact_text_quote: The second mechanism, explainability, enables humans to comprehend the actions of the AI

requirement: The system shall adapt its collaboration mode based on task complexity, switching from augmentation to assemblage for more complex data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: With increasing (task) complexity, human-AI collaboration moves from traditional human-computer interaction to more sophisticated interaction patterns

requirement: The system shall support task delegation between human and AI, allowing users to allocate code generation tasks based on complementary strengths.
exact_text_quote: task delegation is strongly connected to the first two topics; by understanding the strengths, weaknesses, and overarching goal of the task at hand, teams can better allocate their resources to upcoming activities and tasks

requirement: The system shall provide performance monitoring that tracks task progress and provides feedback to users about their collaboration effectiveness.
exact_text_quote: Performance monitoring involves tracking the team member's task progress and ensuring error-free task execution

requirement: The system shall reduce information asymmetry between human users and AI by providing relevant context about the AI's decision-making process.
exact_text_quote: providing information about the inner workings of the AI (i.e., transparency) can positively impact team performance by decreasing information asymmetry

requirement: The system shall support shared mental models by helping users and AI develop a common understanding of data analysis goals.
exact_text_quote: By creating a shared mental model of the team's goal, Schelble et al. (2022) find that a shared understanding of the goal is much more important for human-AI teams than for human teams

requirement: The system shall provide feedback mechanisms that enable users to understand both their own performance and the AI's performance during code generation tasks.
exact_text_quote: The feedback provided to participants encompassed their own performance, the AI's performance, and the degree of alignment between the two

requirement: The system shall support team maintenance activities that improve the functioning of the human-AI collaboration, especially for complex data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: with increasing task complexity, team maintenance (i.e., activities that directly impact the relationship between humans and AI and indirectly impact team performance) becomes more essential for human-AI collaboration

requirement: The system shall allow human users to participate in pre-production tasks such as ideation and planning rather than limiting human involvement to only confirming AI-generated output.
exact_text_quote: Models incorporating human creative input delivered higher content interestingness and overall quality as well as greater task performer satisfaction compared to conditions where humans were limited to confirming AI's output.

requirement: The system shall preserve output diversity by enabling human participation in early creative phases of the analysis workflow.
exact_text_quote: greater human involvement in early creative tasks during the pre-production phase helps counteract this effect

requirement: The system shall enable high-skilled users to maintain creative control over the analysis process rather than restricting them to prompting and judgment roles.
exact_text_quote: allowing human involvement in creative tasks provides more opportunity for high-skilled writers to excel

requirement: The system shall improve user satisfaction by allowing human creative participation rather than limiting humans to confirmation-only roles.
exact_text_quote: Human Creativity and Copilot groups reported higher satisfaction, flexibility, and effectiveness of the process compared to the Human Confirmation group

requirement: The system shall enable users to maintain ownership of the creative process by allowing their involvement in creative tasks.
exact_text_quote: participants felt removed from content creation, struggled with prompting AI to produce content fully aligned with their vision, and found AI-generated stories lacking personal touch, resulting in reduced ownership

requirement: The system shall reduce skill-based performance gaps by providing AI assistance that benefits lower-skilled users.
exact_text_quote: AI reduced skill-based disparities in writing quality, as seen from Day 2 versus Day 1 quality plot (slopes << 1)

requirement: The system shall support iterative refinement through human-AI collaboration throughout the task process.
exact_text_quote: AI helped with diverse storylines and iterative refinement

requirement: The system shall encourage users to explore approaches beyond their personal experience while preserving aggregate diversity through human creative involvement.
exact_text_quote: Increased AI involvement encouraged creators to explore beyond personal experience but also led to lower aggregate diversity in stories and genres among participants. However, this effect was mitigated through human participation in early creative tasks.

requirement: The system shall function as a collaborative partner augmenting human capabilities rather than as a replacement that excludes humans from creative tasks.
exact_text_quote: AI can serve as a copilot, augmenting humans in all tasks, as seen with GitHub Copilot

requirement: The system shall allow users to form an independent preliminary assessment before receiving AI recommendations to avoid anchoring bias.
exact_text_quote: This pattern begins with the user forming an independent preliminary prediction given the decision-making problem. Following this initial judgment, the AI's predicted outcome is presented and may accompanied by support information.

requirement: The system shall allow users to actively seek information or solutions from the AI rather than automatically presenting AI inferences.
exact_text_quote: In this interaction pattern, the user has to actively seek information or solutions from the AI. Rather than the AI's inferences being automatically presented, the user can control when they want to receive the AI assistance.

requirement: The system shall support dialogue-like engagement where users provide information and receive AI guidance through iterative exchange.
exact_text_quote: Within this interaction pattern, the AI facilitates a dialogue-like engagement with the user. Guided by the AI's instructions, the user responds by providing pertinent information. The iterative exchange continues until the AI's instruction requirements are satisfied.

requirement: The system shall allow users to modify AI outputs or provide feedback to shape AI inferences.
exact_text_quote: we included an interaction pattern where humans can modify the outcome space of the AI agent. Typically, information flows from the AI to the user. However, in this pattern, the direction of flow is reversed, with humans providing the AI with feedback, corrections, or information to shape its inferences.

requirement: The system shall support delegation patterns where users can decide whether to retain responsibility or transfer tasks to the AI.
exact_text_quote: In this interaction pattern, both the user and the AI leverage their unique strengths and capabilities to optimize the decision-making outcome. Delegation can be a strategic choice when one agent assesses its counterpart as better equipped for a particular task.

requirement: The system shall present supplementary information such as explanations, uncertainty values, or alternate solutions alongside AI recommendations.
exact_text_quote: Display - Information: The agent presents supplementary evidence (e.g., explanations, uncertainty values, alternate solutions) supporting a solution to the primary decision-making task.

requirement: The system shall support clear communication between humans and AI to foster meaningful collaboration.
exact_text_quote: Likewise, finding the right balance of interactivity between humans and AI systems is not just a matter of enhancing user experience but is essential for achieving clear communication, trustworthiness, and meaningful collaboration.

requirement: The system shall provide AI assistance that does not directly solve the primary task but offers supplementary information for users to interpret.
exact_text_quote: In this pattern, the AI offers information that does not serve as direct solutions to the decision-making problem. The user must interpret this supplementary information as an auxiliary task to determine its relevance and decide how to incorporate it into their primary decision-making process.

requirement: The system shall generate responses based on current inputs and insights from prior interactions to ensure coherent and contextually appropriate outputs.
exact_text_quote: Dynamic Response Generation: The AI plans responses not only based on current inputs but also using insights from prior interactions, ensuring coherent and contextually appropriate conversations.

requirement: The system shall prioritize retrieved information based on relevance to the ongoing task, considering recency, relevance, and importance.
exact_text_quote: Context-Aware Retrieval Model: This model is fine-tuned to prioritize memories based on their relevance to the ongoing conversation, considering factors like recency, relevance, and importance.

requirement: The system shall support customization of its behavior and responses to fit specific user requirements and scenarios.
exact_text_quote: Researchers will have the ability to tailor the AI's functionalities and responses to fit specific research scenarios.

requirement: The system shall manage interactions involving multiple participants, understanding context and maintaining coherent responses within group dynamics.
exact_text_quote: The AI must be adept at managing interactions involving multiple participants. This involves understanding the context of multi-person conversations, accurately attributing statements to the right individuals, and maintaining coherent and relevant responses within the group dynamic.

requirement: The system shall maintain consistent personalized characteristics across various usage scenarios.
exact_text_quote: Customizing AI personas to exhibit a range of human-like behaviors and interactions poses a complex challenge. The AI needs to maintain these personalized characteristics consistently across various scenarios, ensuring the personas remain authentic and relatable to users.

requirement: The system shall provide periodic reflections on interactions and automated behavioral tagging to enhance understanding of user needs.
exact_text_quote: The AI Collaborator offers periodic reflections on the conversations. Behavioral tagging identifies and codes predefined behaviors within the conversation, enhancing the understanding of team interactions and individual behaviors.

requirement: The system shall be designed with cultural sensitivities and ethical considerations to ensure appropriateness and inclusivity.
exact_text_quote: Given the diversity of user groups, especially in global settings, the AI must be designed with cultural sensitivities and ethical considerations in mind. Its behavior and responses need to be appropriate, respectful, and inclusive of cultural differences to ensure universal acceptability and effectiveness.

requirement: The system shall decompose high-level data analysis goals into a structured graph of feasible, concrete coding tasks with clearly defined dependencies.

requirement: The system shall dynamically allocate coding tasks to the most suitable AI agents or human developers based on task requirements, worker skills and availability, and resource constraints.

requirement: The system shall continually track the progress of all workers on their assigned coding tasks, proactively identify and resolve bottlenecks, and ensure synchronized effort across the entire analysis workflow.

requirement: The system shall generate and execute modifications to the task graph in real-time, revising code tasks, adjusting worker roles, or reassigning tasks in response to unexpected events or changing priorities.

requirement: The system shall maintain transparent communication with the data scientist stakeholder, providing regular updates on plans, progress, and potential issues to ensure robust oversight.
exact_text_quote: It must maintain transparent communication with the stakeholder, providing regular updates on plans, progress, and potential issues to ensure robust oversight and enable informed intervention when necessary.

requirement: The system shall optimize for multiple competing objectives including cost, execution time, and quality of data analysis outputs, while adapting to stakeholder preference changes during execution.
exact_text_quote: An optimal policy for the Manager Agent must juggle multiple, often competing objectives such as cost, latency and quality, yet the workflow stakeholder can re-rank these objectives mid-execution.

requirement: The system shall maintain governance and compliance by interpreting natural language constraints and adapting to evolving regulatory requirements without compromising operational effectiveness.
exact_text_quote: These agents must interpret natural language constraints, adapt to evolving regulations, and demonstrate compliance across heterogeneous teams.

requirement: The system shall provide transparency through detailed logs and explicit communication to enable fair responsibility distribution and build user trust.
exact_text_quote: Transparency through detailed logs and explicit communication is critical for building trust and enabling fair responsibility distribution.

requirement: The system shall accept natural language prompts describing intended functionality from users.
exact_text_quote: The user requests an AI programming assistant to generate code using a natural language prompt describing intended functionality.

requirement: The system shall generate a pool of candidate code suggestions based on the user's natural language prompt.
exact_text_quote: The AI programming assistant queries the underlying LLM to generate a pool of candidate code and test suggestions using the natural language prompt provided by the user in step 1.

requirement: The system shall generate tests that distinguish between different code suggestions to clarify ambiguous intent.
exact_text_quote: TiCoder partially formalizes ambiguous intent in natural language prompts by generating a set of tests to distinguish common divergent behaviours in generated code suggestions.

requirement: The system shall execute generated tests on each candidate code suggestion and record pass/fail results.
exact_text_quote: The set of generated tests are executed for each candidate code suggestion generated in step 3.

requirement: The system shall rank tests based on their ability to discriminate among the set of code suggestions.
exact_text_quote: We adopt a discriminative test ranking policy that prioritizes tests that can discriminate best among the set of code suggestions generated by the LLM.

requirement: The system shall request user feedback on generated tests indicating whether each test is consistent with user intent.
exact_text_quote: The top ranked test is then surfaced to the user as a query; asking the user if a test is consistent with the user’s intent.

requirement: The system shall prune code suggestions that do not match user intent based on test execution results.
exact_text_quote: Using the user feedback, the pool of candidate code suggestions is pruned based on the test execution results, i.e. dropping any code suggestions that do not match the users intent.

requirement: The system shall re-rank code suggestions in decreasing order of the number of passing tests.
exact_text_quote: The set of code suggestions is then re-ranked in decreasing order of the number of passing tests.

requirement: The system shall return a set of validated tests and a ranked list of code suggestions consistent with user responses.
exact_text_quote: Finally, the AI programming assistant returns (a) a set of tests that the user has approved or specified the output for, and (b) a ranked list of code suggestions consistent with the user responses.

requirement: The system shall support iterative interaction until predefined termination criteria are satisfied.
exact_text_quote: Interaction steps 3-5 can be repeated for multiple iterations, until a predefined termination criteria (e.g., fixed number of steps, absence of tests) has been satisfied.

requirement: The system shall improve code generation accuracy by at least 38% within 5 user interactions.

requirement: The system shall execute code and run tests to validate generated solutions.

requirement: The system shall manage conversations between users and AI agents, tracking objectives and action outcomes.

requirement: The system shall provide syntax checking capabilities to verify code correctness.

requirement: The system shall enable AI agents to autonomously iterate on code until tests pass or objectives are achieved.

requirement: The system shall operate autonomously without requiring user intervention beyond initial goal-setting.

requirement: The system shall execute operations within a secure environment to ensure user privacy and file security.

requirement: The system shall provide AI agents with comprehensive contextual information required for executing data analysis tasks.

requirement: The system shall support multiple AI agents collaborating on complex data analysis tasks.

requirement: The system shall allow users to define specific objectives for code generation and test generation in data analysis workflows.

requirement: The system shall achieve high Pass@1 rates on code generation tasks to demonstrate effectiveness.
exact_text_quote: In our evaluation, we tested AutoDev on the HumanEval dataset, obtaining promising results with 91.5% and 87.8% of Pass@1 for code generation and test generation respectively.

requirement: The system shall generate data analysis code based on detailed, descriptive prompts provided by researchers.

requirement: The system shall enable researchers to design experimental protocols and invoke bioinformatics tools through a chat-based interface without requiring coding expertise.

requirement: The system shall automate data retrieval, metadata extraction, and workflow generation for data analysis tasks.

requirement: The system shall support iterative refinement of data analysis through self-optimization mechanisms to achieve high task completion rates.

requirement: The system shall integrate with existing tools and frameworks for agentic AI development to support data analysis workflows.
exact_text_quote: AutoGen is a comprehensive framework for managing multi-agent systems. It is centered around the idea of customizable and conversable agents.

requirement: The system shall analyze experimental data to extract meaningful insights and identify patterns.

requirement: The system shall execute the generated Python code in a Python interpreter and capture any execution errors for debugging.
exact_text_quote: The generated code is immediately executed in a Python interpreter. If execution encounters an error, the error message is recorded, and the node is marked as buggy, ending the current execution cycle for that node.

requirement: The system shall generate visualizations from experimental results stored in structured files to summarize and illustrate findings.
exact_text_quote: In the plotting phase, Th e A I Sc i e n t i s t - v 2 reads these stored results and the code, generating visualizations that summarize and illustrate the findings clearly.

requirement: The system shall provide automated feedback on generated visualizations using Vision-Language Models to identify issues with visual clarity.
exact_text_quote: These visualizations are subsequently passed to a Vision-Language Model (VLM) for critique. Any issues flagged by the VLM (such as unclear labels, missing legends, or misleading visualizations) result in the node being marked as buggy.

requirement: The system shall automatically load datasets from Hugging Face Hub using standard loading functions.
exact_text_quote: In Th e A I Sc i e n t i s t - v 2, we prompt the system to leverage Hugging Face Hub whenever possible, automatically downloading required datasets using the standard one-line function (datasets.load_dataset).

requirement: The system shall integrate literature review tools during idea formulation to assess novelty and identify relevant prior work.
exact_text_quote: Crucially, this generalized idea generation phase integrates literature review tools, such as Semantic Scholar, in the loop. The system can query the literature database during the idea formulation process to assess the novelty of a proposed concept and identify relevant prior work.

requirement: The system shall manage experimentation through distinct stages with explicit stopping criteria.
exact_text_quote: we introduce an experiment progress manager agent that coordinates four clearly defined stages of scientific experimentation: Stage 1 Preliminary Investigation... Stage 2 Hyperparameter Tuning... Stage 3 Research Agenda Execution... Stage 4 Ablation Studies.

requirement: The system shall execute multiple experiment variations in parallel to accelerate exploration.
exact_text_quote: An LLM is used to generate the plan and experiment code for each new child node, after which all new nodes are executed concurrently in parallel, significantly accelerating the exploration process.

requirement: The system shall select the best-performing experiment nodes using LLM-based evaluation for progression to subsequent stages.
exact_text_quote: After each stage, the experiment manager selects the best-performing node using a dedicated LLM evaluator (see next section) based on clearly articulated criteria.

requirement: The system shall perform replication runs with different random seeds to calculate statistical measures of experimental outcomes.
exact_text_quote: Replication nodes execute replicates of their parent experiments using different random seeds. Typically, several replication nodes are created to enable the calculation of statistical measures (mean and standard deviation) of experimental outcomes, enhancing result robustness.

requirement: The system shall accept research goals specified in natural language and process them to generate relevant outputs.
exact_text_quote: Given a research goal specified in natural language, the system can search and reason over relevant literature to summarize and synthesize prior work and build on it to propose novel, original research hypotheses and experimental protocols for downstream validations

requirement: The system shall provide grounding for its recommendations by citing relevant literature and explaining the reasoning behind its proposals.
exact_text_quote: The co-scientist provides grounding for its recommendations by citing relevant literature and explaining the reasoning behind its proposals.

requirement: The system shall enable iterative refinement of outputs through recursive self-critique and tool use.
exact_text_quote: the agentic nature of the system enables it to recursively self-critique its output and use tools such as web-search to provide itself with feedback to iteratively refine its hypotheses and research proposals

requirement: The system shall support natural language interaction allowing scientists to specify research goals, provide feedback, and guide the system.
exact_text_quote: Scientists can specify their research goals in simple natural language, including informing the system of desirable attributes for the hypotheses or research proposals it should create and the constraints that the synthesized outputs should satisfy

requirement: The system shall employ multiple specialized agents with distinct roles to handle different aspects of the research process.
exact_text_quote: These specialized agents are enumerated below: Generation agent, Reflection agent, Ranking agent, Proximity agent, Evolution agent, and Meta-review agent

requirement: The system shall use an asynchronous task execution framework for flexible compute scaling.
exact_text_quote: a multi-agent architecture with an asynchronous task execution framework for flexible compute scaling

requirement: The system shall evaluate and rank generated hypotheses using a tournament-based evolution process.
exact_text_quote: a tournament evolution process for self-improving hypotheses generation

requirement: The system shall generate outputs that align with the provided research goals, preferences, and constraints defined by the scientist.
exact_text_quote: The generated outputs must precisely align with the research goals, preferences and constraints defined by the scientist.

requirement: The system shall generate outputs that are free of readily apparent flaws and free of potential contradictions with prior literature.
exact_text_quote: The system outputs should be free of readily apparent flaws. Any potential contradictions with prior literature or established knowledge must be explicitly stated and justified.

requirement: The system shall generate novel hypotheses grounded in prior literature rather than simply synthesizing existing information.
exact_text_quote: A key objective of the co-scientist system is to generate novel hypotheses, conjectures, and research plans grounded in prior literature, rather than simply synthesizing existing information

requirement: The system shall generate outputs that are amenable to empirical validation within specified constraints.
exact_text_quote: The system outputs should be amenable to empirical validation within the constraints specified by the scientist.

requirement: The system shall incorporate safety controls to prevent enabling unsafe, unethical, or harmful research.
exact_text_quote: Safety. The system outputs will be controlled to prevent enabling unsafe, unethical, or harmful research.

requirement: The system shall leverage tools including web search and specialized AI models to improve the grounding and quality of generated outputs.

requirement: The system shall enable scientists to contribute their own hypotheses for inclusion in evaluation alongside system-generated outputs.
exact_text_quote: Scientists can also collaborate and provide feedback in a variety of ways, including directly supplying their own ideas and hypotheses, refining those generated by the system, or using natural language chat to guide the system and ensure alignment with their expertise.

requirement: The system shall maintain a persistent context memory to enable iterative computation and reasoning over long time horizons.
exact_text_quote: In order to enable iterative computation and scientific reasoning over long time horizons, the co-scientist uses a persistent context memory to store and retrieve states of the agents and the system during the course of the computation.

requirement: The system shall use a coding assistant to implement requested features, fix bugs, or refactor code in existing codebases.
exact_text_quote: Aider is an agent framework that is designed to implement requested features, fix bugs, or refactor code in existing codebases.

requirement: The system shall execute code-level changes to experiment templates using a coding assistant.

requirement: The system shall automatically execute planned experiments and handle failures by retrying with code fixes up to four times.
exact_text_quote: We make this process more robust by returning any errors upon a failure or time-out (e.g. experiments taking too long to run) to Aider to fix the code and re-attempt up to four times.

requirement: The system shall create visualizations for experimental results using Python plotting scripts.

requirement: The system shall generate scientific paper write-ups in LaTeX format using recorded notes and figures from experimental results.

requirement: The system shall search for relevant academic references using the Semantic Scholar API to support paper writing.

requirement: The system shall achieve reliable code implementation success rates on real-world GitHub issues.
exact_text_quote: With frontier models it achieves a remarkable success rate of 18.9% on the SWE Bench (Jimenez et al., 2024) benchmark, a collection of real-world GitHub issues.

requirement: The system shall be able to write code that learns in-context to improve performance and robustness.
exact_text_quote: These leverage the language model's ability to learn in-context (Olsson et al., 2022) and can greatly improve its performance, robustness and reliability on many tasks.

requirement: The system shall use chain-of-thought reasoning to improve decision-making during idea generation and experiment planning.

The human reviewer can then approve progression to the next subtask or request the agent repeat the current subtask, providing high-level notes for improvement

requirement: The system shall modify programs using edit commands for code refinement.

requirement: The system shall maintain context across code generation sessions to support iterative data analysis workflows.

requirement: The system shall access external knowledge bases to inform code generation for scientific data analysis.

requirement: The system shall ensure reproducibility of generated data analysis code and results.

requirement: The system shall validate generated code through execution testing and error correction.

requirement: The system shall decompose complex data analysis requests into executable sub-tasks.
exact_text_quote: The planner serves as the architectural core of LLM-based scientific agents, translating high-level research objectives into structured, actionable task sequences that orchestrate tool invocations, memory operations, and verification steps.

requirement: The system shall provide transparent documentation of code generation decisions and execution traces.

requirement: The system shall generate code that integrates with scientific databases and APIs for data retrieval.

requirement: The system shall provide plain language explanations of the reasoning behind generated code, including descriptions of underlying algorithms and function selections.
exact_text_quote: ChatGPT can be employed to summarize and interpret code produced by a bioinformatician to provide a plain language interpretation and summary for the bench scientist, including an assessment of the script's limitations.

requirement: The system shall provide assessments of limitations and potential issues in the generated code to enable users to verify the output.

requirement: The system shall be used for discrete, well-defined, and well-understood tasks to ensure accurate data processing.
exact_text_quote: Used judiciously and for discrete well-defined and well-understood tasks, ChatGPT can speed up data processing and be enabling for scientists with little coding experience.

requirement: The system shall warn users that generated code may contain errors requiring manual verification and debugging.
exact_text_quote: if the user is unable to understand each step of the code to check for valid treatment of the data, the use of this tool is risky

requirement: The system shall warn users that relying on AI-generated programming code can lead to erroneous outputs in the absence of code comprehension.
exact_text_quote: relying on AI generated programming code can lead to erroneous outputs in the absence of code comprehension

requirement: The system shall provide code execution capabilities via Python application programming interfaces to support data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: the agent uses in-context prompts to use tools such as web and documentation search and code execution via Python application programming interface (API)

requirement: The system shall break down complex data analysis problems into manageable subtasks addressed by agents with specialized functions.
exact_text_quote: AI agents can break down a problem into manageable subtasks, which can then be addressed by agents with specialized functions for targeted problem solving and integration of scientific knowledge.

requirement: The system shall integrate machine learning tools to analyze large datasets and navigate hypothesis spaces.
exact_text_quote: AI agents improve the efficiency of routine tasks, automate repetitive processes, and analyze large datasets to navigate hypothesis spaces at a scale and precision that surpasses current human-driven efforts.

requirement: The system shall access information in databases via function calling and application programming interfaces.
exact_text_quote: database agent, AI agent that can access information in databases via function calling and application programming interfaces (APIs)

requirement: The system shall use perception modules to receive and process information from various data modalities.
exact_text_quote: Agents use external tools, such as lab equipment, and have perception modules, such as integrated visual ML tools, to receive information from the environment.

requirement: The system shall store and retrieve relevant information using memory modules to support ongoing data analysis tasks.
exact_text_quote: Memory modules are designed to store long-term and short-term learned knowledge. As agents encounter new situations and acquire data, memory modules get updated with new information.

requirement: The system shall implement reasoning capabilities to plan experiments, make decisions on data analysis approaches, and resolve competing interpretations.
exact_text_quote: Reasoning improves agents' capabilities to plan experiments, make decisions on biological hypotheses, and resolve competing candidate biological mechanisms.

requirement: The system shall adapt to new insights and incorporate the latest scientific findings into data analysis workflows.
exact_text_quote: AI agents should be designed to adapt to new biological insights, incorporate the latest scientific findings, and refine hypotheses based on experimental results.

requirement: The system shall characterize uncertainty in its outputs to support reliable data analysis decisions.
exact_text_quote: An AI agent should be able to formulate biomedical hypotheses, critically evaluate them, characterize their uncertainty, and use that as a driver to acquire and refine its scientific knowledge bases in a way that human scientists can trust.

requirement: The system shall use retrieval-augmented generation to answer questions based on scientific literature.
exact_text_quote: the retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is equipped for AI agents to answer questions based on scientific literature.

requirement: The system shall process multimodal data streams and align inputs with text-based representations for comprehensive analysis.
exact_text_quote: Through representation alignment, data from different modalities are analyzed by modality-specific models to generate representations, such as the visual encoder from CLIP for visual information processing.

requirement: The system shall execute tool-use actions by generating commands in specific formats or querying pre-trained control models.
exact_text_quote: To invoke these tools, AI agents generate commands in specific formats or query pre-trained control models to execute actions.

requirement: The system shall utilize large language models to analyze user queries and devise step-by-step plans that guide the subsequent workflow generation process.

requirement: The system shall generate structured specifications of the user's intended workflow based on the action plan, including Title, Inputs, Outputs, and Activities.
exact_text_quote: we provide a standardized template for intended workflows and instruct the LLM to conduct information extraction guided by the plan through slot filling. As depicted in Fig. 3, this template includes four slots ("Title", "Inputs", "Outputs", and "Activities") to capture the intended workflow's key information.

requirement: The system shall compute semantic matching degrees between user queries and candidate workflows using multiple attributes including Title, Description, Inputs, Outputs, Activities, and Plan.
exact_text_quote: Finally, the semantic matching degree between the user query and candidate workflow is determined by the weighted average of six similarities: MD = α · Sim_title + β · Sim_desc + γ · Sim_Input + δ · Sim_Output + ε · Sim_Act + ξ · Sim_plan

requirement: The system shall leverage few-shot prompting to automatically generate plans without requiring annotated plan corpora or additional training.
exact_text_quote: Benefiting from this planning mechanism, PGM leverages few-shot prompting to autonomously generate plans without requiring annotated plan corpora or additional training, thereby facilitating a better understanding of user intents and instructing subsequent generation and matching processes.

requirement: The system shall decompose complex user requirements into multiple sub-tasks through planning to enable accurate understanding of user intents.
exact_text_quote: To tackle complex problems, humans often employ planning to break down intricate problems and schedule solution steps before implementation.

requirement: The system shall compute set-based similarity for Inputs, Outputs, and Activities attributes using maximum weight matching algorithms.
exact_text_quote: The maximum weight matching (mw) algorithm can be used to solve this problem. Then, the similarity between U_1 and U_2 is normalized by Sim(U_1,U_2)=sum/(|U_1| + |U_2| - sum)

requirement: The system shall employ domain-specific embedding models (e.g., BioLinkBERT for biomedical workflows) for enhanced semantic similarity computation.
exact_text_quote: Since the majority users of scientific workflows are from the biomedical and bioinformatics fields, we employ BioLinkBERT, achieving SOTA performance on several biomedical NLP benchmarks like BLURB, as our basic embedding model.

requirement: The system shall use text-based computation with cosine similarity for Title, Description, and Plan attributes.
exact_text_quote: We employ the fine-tuned BioLinkBERT model to embed each attribute of both the intended workflow and candidate workflow. The semantic similarity between them, denoted as Sim_title, Sim_desc, and Sim_plan respectively, is measured using cosine similarity.

requirement: The system shall maintain robustness to different prompt configurations and exemplar orders when generating plans.
exact_text_quote: The results in Table 2 show relatively small standard deviations in all cases, indicating that the order of prompts does not significantly affect the performance.

requirement: The system shall use retrieval-augmented generation to ground code generation in relevant literature and prior work.
exact_text_quote: To ground idea generation, the agent needs to retrieve papers related to the given research topic, so that it will be aware of related works when generating new ideas.

requirement: The system shall generate multiple candidate code solutions and use a ranking mechanism to identify the best outputs.
exact_text_quote: Our key insight for idea generation is to generate as many candidate ideas as possible. Our intuition is that only a small fraction of all generated ideas might be high-quality, and we should be willing to expend inference-time compute to generate more candidates so that we can later use a reranker to discover the 'diamond in the rough'.

requirement: The system shall use pairwise comparison-based ranking to evaluate and select the best code solutions.
exact_text_quote: We converted the ICLR submissions into our standard project proposal format and randomly paired up accepted and rejected papers and asked LLMs to predict which one is accepted. On this task, Claude-3.5-Sonnet achieves an accuracy of 71.4% with zero-shot prompting.

requirement: The system shall incorporate human expert feedback to improve ranking of generated solutions.
exact_text_quote: Since our AI ranker is still far from perfect, we also introduce another experiment condition where the first author of this paper manually reranked the generated project proposals instead of relying on the LLM ranker, and we call this the AI Ideas + Human Rerank condition.

requirement: The system shall provide structured templates with detailed implementation steps for code solutions.
